**[SCHEDULE B-I --- REQUIREMENTS]{.underline}**

The following are the requirements to be satisfied prior to the issuance of the policy or policies of title insurance described in Schedule A. The Company reserves the right to add additional requirements upon receipt of further information or upon review of the closing documents. All instruments required to be recorded must be filed in the Bridgeport Land Records, Office of the Town Clerk, City of Bridgeport, Connecticut, unless otherwise specified.

**Requirement 1: Deed**

Execution and delivery of a Fiduciary Deed (or Warranty Deed in fiduciary form) conveying the subject property from the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, by and through Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix, to Meridian Cove Properties LLC, a Connecticut limited liability company, in recordable form satisfactory to the Company. The deed must reference the authority of the Executrix as derived from the Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417, and must be accompanied by payment of the Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax. The deed shall convey fee simple title to the land described in Schedule A, Section A-4, together with all improvements, appurtenances, and rights thereto.

**Requirement 2: Probate Authorization**

A certified copy of the Fiduciary Certificate (or Letters Testamentary, as applicable) issued by the Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417, authorizing Claudia Whitford-Barnes as Executrix of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford to sell, convey, and transfer real property, including the subject property, must be presented and recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records. The certificate must be dated no more than sixty (60) days prior to the scheduled closing date. If the Probate Court has issued a specific order authorizing the sale of the subject property, a certified copy of such order must also be provided.

**Requirement 3: Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax Return**

Completion, execution, and filing of the Connecticut Real Estate Conveyance Tax Return (Form OP-236) by both Seller (grantor) and Buyer (grantee), with payment of the applicable conveyance tax. The executed tax return must accompany the deed for recording in the Bridgeport Land Records.

**Requirement 4: Satisfaction / Release of First Mortgage --- Harborstone Federal Credit Union**

A release, satisfaction, or discharge of the first mortgage held by Harborstone Federal Credit Union, originally recorded on March 15, 2015, in the Bridgeport Land Records, Volume 1087, Page 445, securing an original principal indebtedness of \$1,200,000.00, must be obtained and recorded. The payoff amount per the lender\'s payoff statement dated June 10, 2025, is **\$687,412.33** (good through July 20, 2025; per diem interest accrues at the rate of \$112.67 per day thereafter). The release or satisfaction must be in recordable form and must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at or prior to the closing of the transaction.

**Requirement 5: Satisfaction / Release of Home Equity Line of Credit --- Harborstone Federal Credit Union**

A release, satisfaction, or discharge of the Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC) held by Harborstone Federal Credit Union, recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records, Volume 1204, Page 218, with an original credit limit of \$300,000.00, must be obtained and recorded. The payoff amount per the lender\'s payoff statement dated June 10, 2025, is **\$148,219.56** (good through July 20, 2025). The release or satisfaction must be in recordable form and must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at or prior to the closing of the transaction.

**Requirement 6: Release of Mechanic\'s Lien --- Northbridge Construction Co.**

A release of the mechanic\'s lien filed by Northbridge Construction Co. on August 12, 2024, and recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records, Volume 1398, Page 77, in the claimed amount of \$37,500.00, arising from allegedly unpaid renovation work performed on the subject property, must be obtained and recorded. The Company has been advised that the parties have reached a settlement in the amount of \$31,000.00, payable at closing in exchange for a full release and discharge of the mechanic\'s lien. A release in recordable form, executed by Northbridge Construction Co. or its authorized representative, must be obtained and recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at or prior to closing.

**Requirement 7: Release of Connecticut Department of Revenue Services Estate Tax Lien**

A release or certificate of no tax due from the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services (CT DRS) with respect to the estate tax lien filed against the property of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, deceased. The statutory lien was filed as a precautionary measure in connection with the death of Gerald T. Whitford on September 3, 2024, in accordance with Connecticut General Statutes § 12-398. The Company requires recording of a release of this lien in the Bridgeport Land Records as a condition of insuring title to the subject property.

The Company understands that the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford may be below the applicable Connecticut estate tax exemption threshold and that no Connecticut estate tax may ultimately be due. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company requires either: (a) a formal Release of Lien issued by the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services, or (b) a Certificate of No Tax Due (Form CT-706/709 NT or equivalent) issued by the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services, confirming that no Connecticut estate tax is due with respect to the estate of the decedent. The applicable document must be in recordable form and must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at or prior to the closing of the transaction. No payoff amount is associated with this lien.

**Requirement 8: Payment of Delinquent Property Taxes**

Payment of all delinquent real property taxes, including accrued interest and penalties, owed to the City of Bridgeport for the subject property. The title search reveals that the second installment of real property taxes for the fiscal year July 1, 2024 -- June 30, 2025, in the amount of \$26,240.00 (due January 1, 2025), remains unpaid and is delinquent. All delinquent taxes, together with statutory interest at the rate of 1.5% per month (or such other rate as may be applicable under Connecticut General Statutes § 12-146), must be paid in full at or prior to closing. The Company will require a tax status letter or municipal lien certificate from the City of Bridgeport Tax Collector\'s Office confirming that all real property taxes, interest, penalties, and other municipal charges have been satisfied or will be satisfied from the proceeds of closing.

**Requirement 9: Assignment of Leases**

Execution and delivery of an Assignment and Assumption of Leases, in recordable form, with respect to: (a) the commercial lease between Gerald T. Whitford (as landlord) and Coastal Provisions Market LLC (as tenant) for the ground-floor commercial space (approximately 3,200 square feet), with a lease term of January 1, 2021 through December 31, 2030, of which approximately 5.5 years remain; and (b) all residential leases for Units 2A through 2F and Units 3A through 3F, to the extent such units are occupied under written lease agreements. The assignment must assign all of the grantor\'s right, title, and interest in and to the leases and all rents, security deposits, and obligations arising thereunder.

**Requirement 10: Mortgage from Buyer to Lender**

Recording of a first priority mortgage, deed of trust, and security agreement from Meridian Cove Properties LLC, as mortgagor, to Tidewater Savings Bank, as mortgagee, in the principal amount of \$2,640,000.00, together with any required UCC financing statements, assignment of leases and rents (as additional collateral), and related loan security documents, all in recordable form satisfactory to the Company.

**Requirement 11: Entity Verification --- Buyer**

Delivery of the following with respect to the proposed buyer, Meridian Cove Properties LLC:

> \(a\) A Certificate of Legal Existence (or Certificate of Good Standing) for Meridian Cove Properties LLC issued by the Connecticut Secretary of the State, dated within thirty (30) days of the closing date;
>
> \(b\) A copy of the Operating Agreement or other organizational documents of Meridian Cove Properties LLC, authorizing the purchase of the subject property and the execution of all closing documents, and identifying Elaine R. Matsuda as Manager with full authority to bind the entity in connection with this transaction; and
>
> \(c\) A resolution or written consent of the members of Meridian Cove Properties LLC, if required by the Operating Agreement, authorizing the transaction.

**Requirement 12: Survey**

Delivery of a current ALTA/NSPS Land Title Survey of the subject property, prepared by a licensed land surveyor in the State of Connecticut, certified to the Company, the proposed insured (Meridian Cove Properties LLC), and the proposed insured lender (Tidewater Savings Bank), or, in the alternative, an acceptable affidavit of no change with respect to an existing survey previously certified to the Company.

**Requirement 13: Seller\'s Affidavit**

Execution and delivery of a standard owner\'s/seller\'s affidavit by Claudia Whitford-Barnes, in her capacity as Executrix of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, attesting to the absence of any additional liens, encumbrances, judgments, unrecorded interests, boundary disputes, parties in possession (other than tenants under known leases), mechanic\'s lien claims (other than the Northbridge Construction Co. lien identified herein), or other matters affecting title to the subject property that are not shown in the public records. The affidavit must be in form and substance satisfactory to the Company.

**Requirement 14: Gap Indemnity / Closing Protection Letter**

Issuance by the Company of a closing protection letter in favor of the buyer (Meridian Cove Properties LLC) and the lender (Tidewater Savings Bank), together with execution by the appropriate parties of a gap indemnity agreement covering the period between the effective date of the title search (June 16, 2025) and the date of recording of the closing documents in the Bridgeport Land Records.

**[SCHEDULE B-II --- EXCEPTIONS FROM COVERAGE]{.underline}**

The policy or policies to be issued will contain exceptions to the following matters unless the same are disposed of to the satisfaction of the Company prior to the date of closing. The Company reserves the right to add additional exceptions upon receipt of the survey, additional documentation, or updated title search information.

**Part 1: Standard / General Exceptions**

**Exception 1.** Rights or claims of parties in possession not shown by the public records, including specifically, but not limited to, the rights of the following tenants under leases: (a) Coastal Provisions Market LLC, tenant of the ground-floor commercial space; (b) residential tenants of Units 2A, 2B, 2C, 2D, 2E, 2F, 3A, 3B, 3D, 3E, and 3F. Unit 3C has been reported as vacant since on or about November 1, 2024. The Company makes no representation as to the identity or rights of any subtenants, licensees, or other occupants.

**Exception 2.** Easements, or claims of easements, not shown by the public records.

**Exception 3.** Any encroachment, encumbrance, violation, variation, or adverse circumstance affecting the title that would be disclosed by an accurate and complete land survey of the subject property, including, without limitation, matters relating to the location of improvements, fences, walls, boundary lines, and encroachments from or onto adjacent parcels. (Note: This exception may be removed or modified upon receipt and review of a satisfactory ALTA/NSPS Land Title Survey per Requirement 12.)

**Exception 4.** Any lien, or right to a lien, for services, labor, or materials heretofore or hereafter furnished, imposed by law and not shown by the public records, including but not limited to mechanic\'s liens arising under Connecticut General Statutes § 49-33 et seq.

**Exception 5.** Real property taxes and assessments not yet due and payable, including but not limited to taxes for the fiscal year commencing July 1, 2025, and any supplemental assessments or special assessments that may be levied by the City of Bridgeport or any other taxing authority.

**Exception 6.** Any facts, rights, interests, or claims that are not shown by the public records but that could be ascertained by an inspection of the property or by making inquiry of persons in possession thereof.

**Exception 7.** Water and sewer charges billed by the City of Bridgeport Water Pollution Control Authority, which may constitute a lien on the property if unpaid. The Company has not independently verified the status of water and sewer charges; a municipal lien certificate should be obtained to confirm the current status of all such charges.

**Part 2: Special Exceptions (Property-Specific)**

**Exception 8.** The subject property is located in the **MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District)** zone pursuant to the zoning regulations of the City of Bridgeport. The Company does not insure compliance with zoning ordinances, building codes, environmental regulations, or other land use regulations. No assurance is given that the present or intended use of the property conforms to the applicable zoning requirements.

**Exception 9.** Utility easement in favor of the City of Bridgeport, Department of Public Facilities, for the installation, maintenance, repair, and replacement of a sanitary sewer line, as set forth in an instrument recorded in Volume 289, Page 112, Bridgeport Land Records, affecting the northerly eight (8) feet of the subject lot, as more particularly described therein.

**Exception 10.** Utility easement in favor of Coastline Power & Light Company for the installation, maintenance, repair, and replacement of underground electrical conduit and related equipment, as set forth in an instrument recorded in Volume 301, Page 56, Bridgeport Land Records, affecting the easterly five (5) feet of the subject lot, as more particularly described therein.

**Exception 11.** Declaration of Covenants, Conditions, and Restrictions applicable to the Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, recorded in Volume 312, Page 1, Bridgeport Land Records, including but not limited to architectural review requirements, signage restrictions, parking allocation provisions, maintenance obligations, and use restrictions, as the same may have been amended or supplemented from time to time. All terms, provisions, conditions, and obligations set forth therein are excepted from coverage to the extent they affect the subject property.

**Exception 12.** Terms, conditions, and provisions of the commercial lease between Gerald T. Whitford (as landlord) and Coastal Provisions Market LLC (as tenant), dated on or about January 1, 2021, for the ground-floor commercial space, a memorandum of which was recorded in Volume 1285, Page 334, Bridgeport Land Records, including but not limited to any options to renew, rights of first refusal, or other interests of the tenant that may burden the property.

**Exception 13.** Rights of residential tenants under month-to-month or annual lease agreements for Units 2A through 2F and Units 3A through 3F, as disclosed by the rent roll provided by Bayshore Management Co. (Tomás Herrera, Operations Director). Residential leases are unrecorded. The Company makes no representation as to the specific terms, duration, or enforceability of such leases.

**Exception 14.** Management Agreement between the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford and Bayshore Management Co., to the extent the same may affect title to or create any possessory or other rights in the subject property. The Company has been advised that this agreement is being terminated effective at closing; however, the Company excepts from coverage any rights or claims that may arise under or in connection with such agreement.

**Exception 15.** Any state of facts that an accurate and complete survey of the subject property would show regarding the location of improvements, fences, boundary lines, or encroachments to or from adjacent properties.

**[CONDITIONS AND STIPULATIONS]{.underline}**

**1. Definitions. As used in this Commitment: (a) \"Commitment\" means this Commitment for Title Insurance, including all Schedules and attachments hereto; (b) \"Company\" means Continental Hartleigh Title Insurance Company; (c) \"Policy\" means the title insurance policy or policies to be issued pursuant to this Commitment; (d) \"Insured\" means the party or parties identified as the proposed insured in Schedule A; (e) \"Knowledge\" means actual knowledge, not constructive knowledge or notice that may be imputed to a party by reason of the public records or otherwise; (f) \"Land\" means the real property described in Schedule A, Section A-4, including improvements and fixtures located thereon.**

**2. Limitation of Liability.** The liability of the Company under this Commitment shall not exceed the amount of the proposed policy or policies set forth in Schedule A. If two or more policies are to be issued, the aggregate liability of the Company shall not exceed the largest single policy amount unless the policies expressly provide otherwise. The Company shall not be liable for any claim arising out of the status of title to the land prior to the effective date of this Commitment except as expressly set forth herein.

**3. Nature of Commitment.** This Commitment is a contract to issue one or more title insurance policies and is not an abstract of title, a legal opinion, a report on the condition of title, or a guarantee of the status of title. The Company makes no representation regarding the condition of title other than as expressly set forth in this Commitment and in the policy or policies to be issued hereunder.

**4. Satisfaction of Requirements.** The obligation of the Company to issue the policy or policies described in Schedule A is contingent upon the full satisfaction of all requirements set forth in Schedule B-I hereof. The Company reserves the right to add, modify, or delete requirements upon receipt of additional information, updated title search results, or review of closing documents.

**5. Claims Procedure. Any claim under this Commitment or under any policy issued pursuant hereto must be made in writing and delivered to the Company at the office of Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC, 88 Field Point Road, Greenwich, CT 06830, or to Continental Hartleigh Title Insurance Company at its home office address. The claim must include a description of the defect, lien, encumbrance, or other matter giving rise to the claim and such supporting documentation as the Company may reasonably require.**

**6. Countersignature.** This Commitment shall not be valid or binding until countersigned by an authorized officer or agent of Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC.

**7. Extension of Effective Date.** The effective date of this Commitment may be extended only by a written endorsement executed by the Company or its authorized agent.

**8. Arbitration.** To the extent permitted by applicable Connecticut law, any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Commitment may be submitted to binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association then in effect.

**9. Exclusions from Coverage.** The following matters are excluded from the coverage of any policy issued pursuant to this Commitment: (a) any law, ordinance, permit, or governmental regulation relating to the use, occupancy, enjoyment, or improvement of the land, including building and zoning regulations, except to the extent that a notice of enforcement or a violation thereof has been recorded in the public records at the date of the policy; (b) the exercise of the power of eminent domain; (c) defects, liens, encumbrances, adverse claims, or other matters created, suffered, assumed, or agreed to by the insured; (d) defects, liens, encumbrances, adverse claims, or other matters known to the insured and not known to the Company as of the date of the policy and not disclosed in writing to the Company prior to closing; and (e) matters arising after the date of the policy.

**10. Connecticut-Specific Provisions.** In accordance with applicable Connecticut statutes and regulations, the priority of mechanic\'s liens under Connecticut General Statutes § 49-33 et seq. shall be determined as of the date of commencement of services or furnishing of materials, and any policy issued hereunder shall be subject to the provisions of Connecticut law governing the priority and enforcement of such liens.

*--- End of Commitment ---*

**[PRE-CLOSING INSPECTION REPORT AND HEATING OIL TANK READING]{.underline}**

**Prepared in Connection with the Sale and Purchase of** **4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Inspection Date:**                **July 14, 2025**
  ----------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------
  **Report Date:**                    July 14, 2025

  **Prepared For:**                   Meridian Cove Properties LLC (Buyer)

                                      Ridgeline Law Group PLLC (Buyer\'s Counsel)

  **Prepared By:**                    Soundview Property Inspections LLC

                                      Craig D. Ambrose, Licensed Property Inspector

                                      CT License No. HI-2019-03841

                                      47 Seaview Avenue, Bridgeport, CT 06604

  **Reference:**                      Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025

  **Scheduled Closing Date:**         July 15, 2025
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 1: Inspection Summary and Scope]{.underline}**

This report documents a pre-closing walk-through inspection conducted on July 14, 2025, at approximately 10:00 a.m. at the property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604.

**Legal Description.** The property is identified as Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records.

**Property Type.** The subject property is a mixed-use building consisting of a ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet, currently occupied by Coastal Provisions Market LLC, and twelve residential apartments designated as Units 2A through 2F on the second floor and Units 3A through 3F on the third floor.

**Building Characteristics.** The building was originally constructed in 1998 and underwent a partial renovation in 2019. The total building area is approximately 18,400 square feet, situated on a 0.38-acre lot. The property is located within the MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District) zoning classification.

**Purpose of Inspection.** The purpose of this pre-closing inspection is to: (a) document the condition of the property immediately prior to the scheduled closing; (b) verify the completion status of any agreed-upon repairs; (c) confirm the presence and quantity of heating oil in the building\'s storage tank for the purpose of calculating a closing adjustment; and (d) identify any material changes in the condition of the property since the initial due diligence inspection.

**Persons Present During Inspection.**

> • Craig D. Ambrose, Licensed Property Inspector, Soundview Property Inspections LLC
>
> • Elaine R. Matsuda, Manager, Meridian Cove Properties LLC
>
> • Tomás Herrera, Operations Director, Bayshore Management Co. (representing the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford)

**Weather Conditions.** Clear skies, approximately 78°F, dry conditions. No precipitation in the preceding 48 hours.

**[Section 2: Exterior Condition]{.underline}**

**Building Envelope.** The exterior consists of brick masonry on the ground level and fiber-cement siding on the upper floors. Both materials were observed to be in generally acceptable condition. No new cracks, settlement patterns, or structural distress were identified since the due diligence inspection conducted in May 2025. Minor cosmetic paint peeling was observed on the third-floor window trim along the north elevation. This condition was previously noted during due diligence and does not constitute a material deficiency.

**Parking Area and Grounds.** The asphalt parking lot accommodates approximately eighteen vehicles and is in fair overall condition. Two areas of moderate cracking near the southwest corner of the lot were previously identified during the due diligence inspection period; no worsening of these conditions was observed. Landscaping is maintained and in seasonal order. No debris, unauthorized dumping, or evidence of hazardous materials were observed in the exterior areas.

**Windows and Doors.** The ground-floor commercial storefront windows are intact and in good condition. Residential windows were checked on a spot-check basis, including Units 2A, 3B, and 3D; all operated properly, with hardware and weatherstripping in functional condition. The rear service door on the east side of the building closes securely and the lock engages properly.

**Foundation.** Visible portions of the poured-concrete foundation were inspected from grade level and from within the basement. No new cracks, efflorescence, or evidence of water intrusion were observed.

**[Section 3: Roof Condition --- Repair Escrow Items]{.underline}**

**Roof Type.** The primary roof system consists of a modified bitumen flat roof over the main building area. A secondary EPDM (ethylene propylene diene monomer) membrane covers the rear mechanical room extension, which was added during the 2019 renovation.

**Condition.** The inspector confirms that the roof deficiencies identified during the initial due diligence inspection conducted in May 2025 remain **unrepaired** as of the date of this pre-closing inspection. The specific deficiencies are as follows:

> \(a\) **Blistering and Delamination.** Multiple areas of blistering and delamination are present on the modified bitumen membrane along the south-facing section of the roof. Approximately 400 square feet of membrane area is affected. The blistering is consistent with age-related deterioration and UV exposure.
>
> \(b\) **Compromised Flashing.** The flashing at the parapet wall junctions on both the east and west elevations has separated from the substrate in several locations. These gaps are potential points of water ingress during rain events and are considered a significant maintenance deficiency.
>
> \(c\) **Ponding Water Evidence.** Evidence of ponding water was observed near two roof drains located in the northwest and southeast quadrants of the main roof. Residual staining, algae growth, and minor sediment deposits indicate that water does not drain adequately from these areas, suggesting inadequate drainage slope or partially obstructed drain assemblies.
>
> \(d\) **EPDM Seam Separation.** The EPDM membrane over the mechanical room extension exhibits seam separation in two locations, each measuring approximately six to eight linear feet in length. These seam failures compromise the watertight integrity of the membrane and will require professional repair or overlay.

**Leakage Evidence.** Interior evidence of prior water infiltration was observed. Staining is present on ceiling tiles in Unit 3E, which is situated directly below the south-facing roof section, and in the third-floor common hallway adjacent to that unit. The staining appears consistent with conditions previously documented during the due diligence period. No evidence of active leaking was observed on the date of inspection, which is attributable to the dry weather conditions at the time.

**Note Regarding Repair Escrow.** Per Section 8.4 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025, the parties have agreed that the sum of Forty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$45,000.00) shall be deposited by the Seller into escrow at closing with Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC to fund the remediation of the roof deficiencies described above. As of this inspection date, the repairs have not been commenced. The inspector recommends that the Buyer obtain competitive contractor estimates promptly after closing to confirm that the escrow amount is adequate to cover the full scope of necessary roof work, including membrane replacement, flashing repair, drainage correction, and EPDM seam repair.

**Estimated Useful Life of Roof (Absent Repairs).** Based on current conditions, the inspector estimates that the modified bitumen sections have approximately one to two years of remaining useful life without intervention. The EPDM membrane over the mechanical room extension may fail sooner if the seam separations are not addressed, particularly during freeze-thaw cycles in the coming winter season.

**[Section 4: Interior Condition --- Common Areas]{.underline}**

**Lobbies and Hallways.** The first-floor lobby is in good condition, with tile flooring, painted drywall, and adequate lighting. The second- and third-floor hallways are in acceptable condition. Carpeting in the hallways shows normal wear consistent with the building\'s age and occupancy levels. Overhead lighting fixtures were tested and found to be functional throughout all common corridors.

**Stairwells.** The building has two stairwells --- a front stairwell accessed from the main lobby and a rear stairwell accessed from the east service corridor. Both stairwells are in acceptable condition. Handrails are securely fastened and continuous. Exit signage is illuminated and visible at all required locations. Treads and risers are in sound condition with no tripping hazards observed.

**Mechanical Room.** The basement mechanical room houses the building\'s primary heating and hot water systems. The oil-fired hydronic boiler was visually inspected and appears functional, with no visible leaks, unusual corrosion, or signs of malfunction. The hot water system consists of a gas-fired, 100-gallon commercial water heater that is in operable condition. The main electrical panel is labeled and accessible, with no evidence of overheating, scorching, or improper modifications.

**Laundry Room (Second Floor).** The second-floor laundry room contains two washing machines and two clothes dryers, all of which were in place and appeared operational based on a visual check. No leaks or drainage issues were noted.

**Unit 3C (Vacant).** The inspector accessed vacant Unit 3C with the cooperation of Bayshore Management Co. The unit is in broom-clean condition. No evidence of damage, pest infestation, or moisture intrusion was observed. All appliances are in place, including a refrigerator, range/oven, and dishwasher. The unit appears turnkey-ready for leasing upon the Buyer\'s acquisition of the property.

**[Section 5: Interior Condition --- Commercial Space (Ground Floor)]{.underline}**

**Tenant.** The ground-floor commercial space is occupied by Coastal Provisions Market LLC, a specialty grocery and market operated by Ryan and Sara Mulgrew.

**Observation.** The ground-floor commercial space, comprising approximately 3,200 square feet, was observed to be open and conducting normal business operations at the time of the inspection. The inspector conducted a visual walk-through of the space with the cooperation of on-site staff. Ms. Mulgrew was not present during the walk-through, but the store manager permitted access for the limited purposes of this inspection.

**Condition.** The commercial space is in good overall condition. Tenant improvements, including refrigerated display cases, shelving, and the point-of-sale area, appear well-maintained. No visible structural issues, water damage, or apparent building code violations were observed. The HVAC system serving the commercial space, which operates from a separate rooftop unit independent of the building\'s hydronic heating system, was operating normally and maintaining comfortable interior temperatures.

**Note.** The inspector did not conduct a detailed inspection of tenant-owned fixtures and equipment, which constitute the personal property of the tenant and are not being conveyed in this transaction. This report addresses only the building systems, structural elements, and landlord-owned improvements within the commercial space.

**[Section 6: Heating Oil Tank Reading]{.underline}**

**Heating System.** The building is heated by an oil-fired hydronic boiler system that provides hot water heat to the twelve residential apartments and to the common areas. (The ground-floor commercial space is served by a separate HVAC system and does not draw on the oil-fired boiler.) Heating oil for the boiler is stored in an above-ground storage tank located in the basement mechanical room.

**Tank Description.** The storage tank is a 275-gallon steel above-ground storage tank of single-wall construction. The tank is equipped with a standard float-style fuel gauge and is positioned on a level concrete pad in the mechanical room. The tank was visually inspected and appears to be in acceptable condition, with no visible corrosion, leaks, seepage, or staining on the concrete floor beneath or around the tank. The fuel supply line from the tank to the boiler is copper tubing with a shut-off valve, and no leaks were observed along the supply line.

**Gauge Reading.** At the time of inspection, on July 14, 2025, at approximately 11:15 a.m., the tank gauge indicated approximately **180 gallons** of heating oil remaining. The inspector visually confirmed the gauge reading and independently verified the approximate fill level by tapping the tank exterior at multiple levels to assess the liquid line. The tapping verification was consistent with the gauge reading of approximately 180 gallons.

**Seller\'s Last Oil Delivery Receipt.** During the inspection, Tomás Herrera of Bayshore Management Co. provided the inspector with a copy of the most recent heating oil delivery receipt for the property. The details of that receipt are as follows:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**                            **Detail**
  ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------
  **Vendor**                          Shoreline Fuel & Oil Co.

  **Delivery Date**                   June 2, 2025

  **Quantity Delivered**              125.0 gallons

  **Product**                         #2 Heating Oil

  **Price Per Gallon**                \$3.85

  **Total Charge**                    \$481.25

  **Account Holder**                  Estate of Gerald T. Whitford / Bayshore Management Co. (agent)
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A copy of the Shoreline Fuel & Oil Co. delivery receipt dated June 2, 2025, is appended to this report as **Attachment A**.

**Calculation for Settlement Purposes.** Based on the tank reading of approximately 180 gallons at the Seller\'s last-delivered price of \$3.85 per gallon, the value of the heating oil remaining in the tank is calculated as follows:

> 180 gallons × \$3.85 per gallon = **\$693.00**

**Application to Settlement Statement.** Per Section 7.6 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025, the Buyer shall reimburse the Seller at closing for heating oil remaining in the tank at the Seller\'s last purchase price per gallon. Accordingly, the amount of \$693.00 should be reflected on the settlement statement as a **credit to the Seller** and a corresponding **charge to the Buyer**.

**[Section 7: Summary of Findings and Recommendations]{.underline}**

**Overall Condition.** The property at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604, is in substantially the same condition as observed during the initial due diligence inspection period in May 2025, with the exception of normal wear and seasonal changes. No material adverse changes in the condition of the building, its systems, or the site were identified during this pre-closing walk-through.

**Items Requiring Attention at Closing:**

> 1\. **Roof Deficiencies** --- The roof deficiencies detailed in Section 3 of this report remain unrepaired and are addressed by the \$45,000.00 repair escrow to be funded by the Seller at closing under Section 8.4 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement.
>
> 2\. **Heating Oil Credit** --- Approximately 180 gallons of heating oil remain in the storage tank, valued at \$693.00 based on the Seller\'s last purchase price of \$3.85 per gallon. This amount is to be credited to the Seller at closing per Section 7.6 of the Purchase and Sale Agreement.

**Items Not Requiring Attention / Informational Only:**

> • Unit 3C remains vacant and in turnkey condition, ready for leasing.
>
> • Minor cosmetic paint peeling on the third-floor window trim (north elevation) is not a material condition and does not require action prior to closing.
>
> • Parking lot cracking near the southwest corner, previously noted during due diligence, has not worsened and is not a structural concern at this time.

**Recommendation.** Based on the observations set forth in this report, the inspector recommends proceeding with the scheduled closing. The repair escrow of \$45,000.00 adequately addresses the most significant deferred maintenance item, which is the roof system. The Buyer should prioritize obtaining competitive contractor bids for the roof work promptly after closing to confirm that the escrow funds are sufficient and to commence repairs before the onset of the fall and winter weather season.

**[Section 8: Certification]{.underline}**

I, Craig D. Ambrose, hereby certify that I personally conducted the pre-closing inspection described in this report on July 14, 2025, at the property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604. The observations, findings, and recommendations set forth herein are accurate and complete to the best of my professional knowledge and judgment. This report has been prepared solely for the benefit of the parties identified herein and their legal counsel in connection with the transaction referenced above.

Whitford (the \"Estate\"), acting through its duly appointed Executrix, Claudia Whitford-Barnes, with respect to the mechanic\'s lien filed by Northbridge against the real property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604 (the \"Property\").

**Background.** In 2019, Northbridge Construction Co. performed interior renovation work at the Property pursuant to a written contract with Gerald T. Whitford. The scope of work included kitchen remodeling, bathroom upgrades, and associated carpentry, plumbing, and electrical subcontractor work. A dispute arose regarding the final invoice for work completed under the contract, and a balance remained unpaid.

On August 12, 2024, Northbridge filed a Certificate of Mechanic\'s Lien against the Property in the Bridgeport Land Records in the total amount of **\$37,500.00**, representing the unpaid balance claimed due and owing for the completed renovation work. The mechanic\'s lien was recorded at Volume 1064, Page 517 of the Bridgeport Land Records. Gerald T. Whitford subsequently passed away on September 3, 2024. The dispute regarding the outstanding balance has since been addressed through correspondence and negotiation between Northbridge and the Estate, through your office.

**Settlement Terms.** After good-faith negotiation, Northbridge and the Estate have agreed to resolve the mechanic\'s lien claim on the following terms:

1\. **Original Lien Amount:** \$37,500.00

2\. **Agreed Settlement / Payoff Amount:** **\$31,000.00**, representing a reduction of \$6,500.00 from the originally claimed amount in full and final satisfaction of the lien and all claims arising from the 2019 renovation contract.

3\. **Payment Terms:** The settlement amount of \$31,000.00 shall be paid at the closing of the sale of the Property, currently scheduled for July 15, 2025. Payment shall be made via wire transfer or certified check, as set forth below.

4\. **Release of Lien:** Upon receipt and confirmation of the \$31,000.00 settlement payment, Northbridge Construction Co. will execute and deliver a Release of Mechanic\'s Lien in recordable form to Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC for recording in the Bridgeport Land Records. Northbridge has prepared the release instrument in advance and will deliver the signed original to your office or directly to the title company at or prior to closing.

5\. **Recording Costs:** The recording fee for the Release of Mechanic\'s Lien shall be the responsibility of the seller (Estate) and is not included in the \$31,000.00 settlement amount.

**Payment Instructions.** The settlement payment of \$31,000.00 may be remitted by either of the following methods:

**Option A --- Wire Transfer:**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Field**                           **Detail**
  ----------------------------------- --------------------------------------
  **Receiving Bank:**                 Stratford Savings Bank

  **ABA Routing Number:**             221172054

  **Account Number:**                 4401-228-6693

  **Account Name:**                   Northbridge Construction Co.

  **Reference Line:**                 Whitford Mechanic\'s Lien Settlement
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Option B --- Certified / Bank Check:**

A certified check or bank check payable to **\"Northbridge Construction Co.\"** may be delivered at closing in lieu of a wire transfer.

For any questions regarding payment, please contact **Vincent Marchetti**, President, at (203) 612-4400 or by email at vmarchetti@northbridgeconstruction.com.

**Mutual Release.** Upon receipt of the \$31,000.00 settlement payment, Northbridge Construction Co. hereby agrees to release and forever discharge the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, its Executrix Claudia Whitford-Barnes, and their respective heirs, successors, assigns, agents, and attorneys, as well as any purchaser of the Property, from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, liens, debts, and obligations of every kind and nature, whether known or unknown, arising from or in any way related to the 2019 renovation contract and the work performed at the Property. This settlement constitutes full and final satisfaction of the mechanic\'s lien recorded at Volume 1064, Page 517 of the Bridgeport Land Records, and Northbridge shall have no further claim against the Property or the Estate with respect thereto.

This agreement is contingent upon receipt of the settlement funds at or before the closing of the sale of the Property. If the closing does not occur by August 31, 2025, Northbridge reserves the right to withdraw this settlement offer and pursue its mechanic\'s lien claim in full.

We appreciate the professional manner in which you and the Estate have handled this matter and look forward to its resolution at closing.

NORTHBRIDGE CONSTRUCTION CO.

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Vincent Marchetti President

Date: July 2, 2025

**[SUMMARY OF EXISTING LIENS AND PAYOFF AMOUNTS]{.underline}**

**Prepared by Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP**

**As of July 10, 2025**

For the convenience of the title company and buyer\'s counsel, the following summary table sets forth all existing liens of record against the Property at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604, together with the applicable payoff amounts, good-through dates, and per diem interest rates:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Lien**                                                                         **Recorded Reference**   **Payoff Amount**                 **Good Through**   **Per Diem**
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------ --------------------------------- ------------------ ---------------
  Harborstone Federal Credit Union --- First Mortgage (Loan No. 2015-MTG-008174)   Vol. 312, Pg. 88         \$687,412.33                      July 20, 2025      \$112.67/day

  Harborstone Federal Credit Union --- HELOC (Loan No. 2018-HEL-003291)            Vol. 487, Pg. 112        \$148,219.56                      July 20, 2025      \~\$27.93/day

  Northbridge Construction Co. --- Mechanic\'s Lien                                Vol. 1064, Pg. 517       \$31,000.00                       At closing         N/A

  CT Dept. of Revenue Services --- Estate Tax Lien                                 Vol. 1087, Pg. 234       **\$0.00** (no payoff required)   N/A                N/A

  **Total Lien Payoffs**                                                                                    **\$866,631.89**                                     
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Note:** The Connecticut Department of Revenue Services estate tax lien requires only a release to be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records; no payoff funds are due or payable. The Estate of Gerald T. Whitford has been determined to fall below the Connecticut estate tax filing threshold, and the DRS has confirmed that the estate tax obligation is \$0.00. A release instrument has been obtained from the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services and will be delivered to Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC for recording at seller\'s expense. No payoff letter accompanies this compilation for the DRS lien because no payoff amount exists.

All payoff figures stated above are subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the individual payoff letters enclosed herewith. Please direct any questions to the undersigned.

ASHFORD, CLEMENT & PAIGE LLP

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

David H. Clement Partner Telephone: (203) 547-8100, ext. 412

Email: dclement@ashfordclement.com

This compilation of payoff letters is provided for use in connection with the closing of the sale of 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604. The individual payoff letters enclosed herein were issued by the respective lienholders and are reproduced without modification. Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP makes no independent representation as to the accuracy of the payoff figures stated by the lienholders, except that counsel has reviewed each letter for consistency with the loan records and lien information available to the Estate. All payoff amounts should be confirmed with the respective payoff contacts prior to disbursement.

**TIDEWATER SAVINGS BANK** *Established 1946*

3100 Post Road Fairfield, Connecticut 06824 Tel: (203) 555-7100 \| Fax: (203) 555-7109 www.tidewaterct.bank NMLS #401562

**Attention:** Elaine R. Matsuda, Manager

**From:** Marcus J. Pellegrino, Loan Officer Commercial Real Estate Lending Division Tidewater Savings Bank

**Re: Commitment to Lend --- Acquisition Financing for 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604** **Loan No. TSB-2025-CRE-04183**

Dear Ms. Matsuda:

**[COMMITMENT LETTER AND CLOSING INSTRUCTIONS]{.underline}**

**[Section 1: Commitment Overview and Recitals]{.underline}**

Tidewater Savings Bank, a Connecticut-chartered savings bank with its principal office located at 3100 Post Road, Fairfield, Connecticut 06824 (\"Lender\"), is pleased to issue this commitment letter (this \"Commitment\") to Meridian Cove Properties LLC, a Connecticut limited liability company formed on January 8, 2024, with its principal place of business in New Haven, Connecticut (\"Borrower\"), for the purpose of financing the acquisition of that certain mixed-use commercial and residential property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604 (the \"Property\").

This Commitment is issued in connection with the purchase and sale agreement dated April 22, 2025 (the \"Purchase Agreement\"), entered into between Borrower, as purchaser, and the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix (the \"Seller\"), pursuant to which Borrower has agreed to acquire the Property for a purchase price of Three Million Nine Hundred Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$3,900,000.00). The scheduled closing date under the Purchase Agreement is July 15, 2025. Lender acknowledges that the financing contingency contained in Section 5.2 of the Purchase Agreement was irrevocably waived by Borrower as of June 10, 2025, and that this Commitment is issued in connection with Borrower\'s unconditional obligation to close the acquisition on or before the scheduled closing date.

The Property is a mixed-use building situated on approximately 0.38 acres, consisting of ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet currently occupied by Coastal Provisions Market LLC, a Connecticut limited liability company, as tenant, and twelve (12) residential apartment units located on the second and third floors. The total building area is approximately 18,400 square feet. The Property is zoned MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District) under the Bridgeport Zoning Regulations. The Property is more particularly described by its legal description as Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records, and as more fully set forth in Exhibit A attached hereto.

Lender has completed its underwriting review, including analysis of Borrower\'s financial condition, the Property\'s income and expenses, and the independent appraisal of the Property, and has determined that the proposed loan satisfies Lender\'s lending criteria, subject to the terms, conditions, and requirements set forth in this Commitment.

**[Section 2: Loan Terms]{.underline}**

The loan to be made by Lender to Borrower (the \"Loan\") shall be made on the following terms and conditions:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Term**                            **Description**
  ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Borrower**                        Meridian Cove Properties LLC, a Connecticut limited liability company

  **Guarantor**                       Elaine R. Matsuda, individually (personal guaranty required as a condition of the Loan, standard for LLC borrowers)

  **Loan Amount**                     Two Million Six Hundred Forty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$2,640,000.00)

  **Loan-to-Value Ratio**             Approximately 67.7%, based on the appraised value of the Property. Lender\'s independent appraisal, completed on May 28, 2025, by Ashford Valuation Associates LLC, determined an as-is market value of Three Million Nine Hundred Twenty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$3,920,000.00), which exceeds the purchase price. The Loan amount of \$2,640,000.00 divided by the appraised value of \$3,920,000.00 yields a loan-to-value ratio of 67.35%, rounded to approximately 67.7% for purposes of this Commitment.

  **Interest Rate**                   6.875% per annum, fixed for seven (7) years from the date of closing

  **Amortization**                    Thirty (30) years, fully amortizing schedule

  **Loan Term / Maturity**            Seven (7) years from the closing date; balloon payment of the entire remaining principal balance shall be due and payable at maturity

  **Monthly Payment**                 Monthly installments of principal and interest, calculated based on a thirty (30) year amortization schedule at the fixed rate of 6.875% per annum, as calculated per the amortization schedule to be provided at closing

  **Payment Commencement**            First monthly payment due on August 15, 2025

  **Prepayment**                      No prepayment of principal shall be permitted during the first twenty-four (24) months following the closing date (lockout period). After the second anniversary of the Loan, prepayment of principal shall be permitted subject to the following prepayment premiums: 2% of the amount prepaid during years 3 and 4; 1% of the amount prepaid during years 5 and 6; and no prepayment premium during year 7.

  **Collateral**                      First priority mortgage lien on the Property (4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604); assignment of all rents and leases; assignment of all tenant security deposits held in connection with the Property

  **Loan Type**                       Commercial real estate acquisition loan
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 3: Borrower\'s Equity Requirement]{.underline}**

Borrower must contribute equity in the amount of no less than One Million Two Hundred Sixty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$1,260,000.00), representing the difference between the purchase price of \$3,900,000.00 and the Loan amount of \$2,640,000.00.

Borrower has deposited earnest money totaling One Hundred Ninety-Five Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$195,000.00) with Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC, as escrow agent under the Purchase Agreement, consisting of Earnest Deposit No. 1 in the amount of \$100,000.00 deposited on April 24, 2025, and Earnest Deposit No. 2 in the amount of \$95,000.00 deposited on May 22, 2025. These earnest money deposits will be applied toward the purchase price at closing and shall count toward satisfaction of the equity requirement.

The balance of Borrower\'s equity contribution, net of the earnest money deposits and adjusted for prorations, credits, and closing costs as reflected on the final settlement statement, must be delivered by Borrower at closing in the form of a wire transfer of immediately available funds to the escrow or trust account of Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC. No personal checks, cashier\'s checks, or other forms of payment shall be accepted for the equity contribution.

**[Section 4: Loan Fees and Charges]{.underline}**

The following fees and charges are associated with the Loan. This schedule is provided for the benefit of Borrower and the settlement agent in preparing the settlement statement.

**4.1 Fees Payable at Closing**

The following fees shall be paid at closing from Borrower\'s funds and shall appear as charges to Borrower on the settlement statement:

**Loan Origination Fee:** One percent (1.00%) of the Loan amount, equal to \$2,640,000.00 × 0.01 = **\$26,400.00**. This fee is payable to Tidewater Savings Bank at closing and shall appear on the settlement statement as a charge to Borrower.

**Flood Certification Fee:** **\$25.00**, payable to CoreLogic Flood Services for FEMA flood zone determination. Payable at closing as a charge to Borrower.

**Tax Service Fee:** **\$85.00**, payable to Tidewater Savings Bank\'s designated tax service provider for ongoing monitoring of real estate tax payments throughout the term of the Loan. Payable at closing as a charge to Borrower.

**Lender\'s Title Insurance Premium:** **\$3,850.00** (simultaneous issue rate). The lender\'s title insurance policy shall be issued by Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC in the amount of \$2,640,000.00 naming Tidewater Savings Bank as the insured lender. Payable at closing as a charge to Borrower.

**Mortgage Recording Fee:** **\$113.00**, payable to the Bridgeport Town Clerk for the recording of the mortgage deed in the Bridgeport Land Records. Payable at closing as a charge to Borrower.

**4.2 Fees Paid Outside of Closing (POC)**

The following fees have been paid by Borrower prior to closing and outside of the closing transaction. These fees are reflected here for disclosure purposes only and must not be included in the calculation of Borrower\'s cash required to close.

**Appraisal Fee: \$4,500.00 --- Paid Outside of Closing (POC).** The appraisal fee in the amount of Four Thousand Five Hundred and 00/100 Dollars (\$4,500.00) was paid directly by Borrower to the Lender\'s designated appraiser, Ashford Valuation Associates LLC, on May 5, 2025, prior to closing. This fee has been paid in full outside of the closing transaction. **This amount should not be included in the Borrower\'s cash required to close and should not appear as a debit or charge on the settlement statement affecting the Borrower\'s balance.** If the settlement statement includes a line item for the appraisal fee for disclosure purposes, it must be clearly marked as \"POC --- Paid Outside of Closing\" with a \$0.00 impact on the Borrower\'s cash-to-close calculation. **Do not include this fee in the computation of funds due from Borrower at closing.**

**CLOSING AGENT INSTRUCTION:** The \$4,500.00 appraisal fee is POC. It was collected on May 5, 2025. It must NOT be added to Borrower\'s closing costs or reflected as an amount due at the closing table. Settlement agents and all parties preparing or reviewing the settlement statement are directed to ensure that this fee does not inflate the Borrower\'s total charges or the funds required from Borrower.

**4.3 Summary of Loan-Related Closing Costs**

The following table summarizes all loan-related fees and charges:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Fee**                             **Amount**              **Payable At / Status**
  ----------------------------------- ----------------------- ------------------------------
  Loan Origination Fee (1%)           \$26,400.00             At Closing

  Flood Certification Fee             \$25.00                 At Closing

  Tax Service Fee                     \$85.00                 At Closing

  Lender\'s Title Insurance Premium   \$3,850.00              At Closing

  Mortgage Recording Fee              \$113.00                At Closing

  Appraisal Fee                       \$4,500.00              **POC --- Paid May 5, 2025**

  **Total At Closing**                **\$30,473.00**         

  **Total Including POC**             **\$34,973.00**         
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Note:** Only the \"Total At Closing\" amount of \$30,473.00 should be reflected in the Borrower\'s cash-to-close calculation on the settlement statement. The appraisal fee of \$4,500.00 designated as POC has already been paid and must not be included in any computation of amounts due from Borrower at closing.

**[Section 5: Conditions Precedent to Closing and Disbursement]{.underline}**

Lender\'s obligation to fund and disburse the Loan proceeds is subject to the satisfaction of each of the following conditions precedent on or prior to the closing date. Lender reserves the right to waive any condition in its sole discretion, and any such waiver shall not constitute a waiver of any other condition.

1\. **Title Insurance.** Lender shall have received a commitment for a lender\'s title insurance policy from Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC (Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer) in the amount of \$2,640,000.00, insuring the first-priority lien position of Lender\'s mortgage on the Property, subject only to the standard printed exceptions contained in the American Land Title Association (ALTA) loan policy form and such other encumbrances as have been reviewed and approved by Lender. An owner\'s title insurance policy in the amount of \$3,900,000.00 must also be issued simultaneously at closing for the benefit of Borrower.

2\. **Satisfaction of Existing Liens.** All existing liens, encumbrances, and security interests affecting the Property must be satisfied, released, or otherwise discharged at or prior to closing, including but not limited to the following:

> \(a\) First mortgage held by Harborstone Federal Credit Union (payoff amount: \$687,412.33, good through July 20, 2025);
>
> \(b\) Home equity line of credit held by Harborstone Federal Credit Union (payoff amount: \$148,219.56, good through July 20, 2025);
>
> \(c\) Mechanic\'s lien filed by Northbridge Construction Co. on the Bridgeport Land Records (agreed settlement amount: \$31,000.00, to be paid from Seller\'s proceeds at closing);
>
> \(d\) Connecticut Department of Revenue Services estate tax lien (Lender understands that no monetary payoff is required as the estate is below the Connecticut estate tax exemption threshold; however, a formal release of the estate tax lien must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records).
>
> Lender requires evidence that all lien releases and satisfactions have been recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records or are in recordable form and will be submitted for recording immediately following the closing.

3\. **Property Taxes.** Evidence satisfactory to Lender that all real property taxes and assessments due and payable through the closing date are current or will be paid at closing from Seller\'s proceeds. Lender is aware that the second installment of the 2024--2025 fiscal year property taxes in the amount of \$26,240.00, plus applicable statutory interest, is currently delinquent and must be paid from Seller\'s proceeds at closing or otherwise satisfied prior to disbursement.

4\. **Survey and Flood Determination.** A current ALTA/NSPS land title survey or a survey endorsement to the title insurance policy confirming no encroachments, boundary discrepancies, or other survey defects. A FEMA flood zone determination letter confirming that the Property is not located within a Special Flood Hazard Area, or if the Property is determined to be within a flood zone, evidence of flood insurance meeting Lender\'s requirements.

5\. **Insurance.** Borrower shall provide evidence of a property and casualty insurance policy covering the Property, naming Tidewater Savings Bank as mortgagee and loss payee pursuant to a standard mortgagee clause, with coverage of not less than \$3,900,000.00 replacement cost value, issued by a carrier with an A.M. Best rating of A- or better.

6\. **Environmental.** A Phase I Environmental Site Assessment of the Property, prepared in accordance with ASTM E1527-21 standards, reflecting no recognized environmental conditions, or if such conditions exist, evidence of remediation or mitigation satisfactory to Lender in its sole discretion.

7\. **Commercial Lease Review.** Lender has reviewed and approved the lease for the ground-floor commercial tenant, Coastal Provisions Market LLC (lease term: January 1, 2021, through December 31, 2030, with approximately four and one-half years remaining). Lender requires execution and delivery of a Subordination, Non-Disturbance, and Attornment Agreement (SNDA) between Lender, Borrower, and Coastal Provisions Market LLC prior to or at closing.

8\. **Residential Lease Review.** Copies of all residential leases for the occupied residential units (Units 2A, 2B, 2C, 2D, 2E, 2F, 3A, 3B, 3D, 3E, and 3F, excluding currently vacant Unit 3C) have been provided to and are acceptable to Lender.

9\. **Assignment of Rents and Leases.** Borrower shall execute and deliver an Assignment of Rents and Leases in recordable form, assigning to Lender all of Borrower\'s right, title, and interest in and to all leases, rents, profits, and income derived from the Property, to be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records.

10\. **Personal Guaranty.** Elaine R. Matsuda shall execute and deliver an unconditional personal guaranty of payment and performance of all obligations arising under the Loan, in form and substance satisfactory to Lender and Lender\'s counsel.

11\. **Entity Documentation.** Borrower shall deliver to Lender certified copies of the following: (a) the Operating Agreement of Meridian Cove Properties LLC; (b) the Certificate of Organization filed with the Connecticut Secretary of the State; (c) a resolution of the members of Meridian Cove Properties LLC authorizing the borrowing, the execution and delivery of the Loan documents, and the grant of the mortgage lien; and (d) a Certificate of Legal Existence dated no earlier than thirty (30) days prior to closing, issued by the Connecticut Secretary of the State.

12\. **Fiduciary Authorization.** With respect to the Seller being the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, Lender requires a certified copy of the fiduciary certificate authorizing Claudia Whitford-Barnes as Executrix to convey real property, issued by the Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417, dated no earlier than sixty (60) days prior to closing.

13\. **Repair Escrow.** Lender acknowledges that, pursuant to Section 8.4 of the Purchase Agreement, the sum of Forty-Five Thousand and 00/100 Dollars (\$45,000.00) will be deposited into a repair escrow account held by Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC at closing for the purpose of funding necessary roof repairs to the Property. Lender requires that the Repair Escrow Agreement be provided for Lender\'s review prior to closing and that Tidewater Savings Bank be named as an interested party therein. Repair escrow funds shall be disbursed only upon Lender\'s prior written consent, following Lender\'s receipt of satisfactory evidence that the repairs have been completed in a workmanlike manner and in compliance with applicable building codes.

**[Section 6: Closing Instructions to Settlement Agent]{.underline}**

The following instructions are directed to Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC (Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer) and to Borrower\'s counsel, Nora F. Kapadia, Esq., Ridgeline Law Group PLLC, regarding the mechanics of the closing.

**6.1 Disbursement of Loan Proceeds**

Loan proceeds in the amount of \$2,640,000.00 shall be wire transferred from Tidewater Savings Bank to the escrow or trust account of Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC on or before the closing date of July 15, 2025. The wire transfer shall be initiated upon Lender\'s confirmation that all conditions precedent to closing and disbursement set forth in Section 5 of this Commitment have been satisfied or waived by Lender. Loan proceeds shall not be disbursed by the settlement agent to any party until Lender has authorized disbursement in writing or by electronic confirmation delivered to Lorraine M. Grasso. Lender\'s wire transfer instructions and account details for the return of any overage or excess funds will be provided under separate cover no later than July 10, 2025.

**6.2 Settlement Statement Review**

A copy of the proposed settlement statement, in substantially final form, must be submitted to Marcus J. Pellegrino, Loan Officer, Tidewater Savings Bank, by email at mpellegrino@tidewaterct.bank and by facsimile to (203) 555-7109, no later than three (3) business days prior to the closing date for Lender\'s review and approval. Lender reserves the right to require modifications to the settlement statement prior to authorizing disbursement.

The settlement statement must accurately reflect the Loan amount of \$2,640,000.00 as a credit to Borrower. All lender-related fees and charges set forth in Section 4 of this Commitment must be accurately reflected on the settlement statement in the proper amounts and payable to the correct payees.

**IMPORTANT --- APPRAISAL FEE --- POC INSTRUCTION:** The appraisal fee of \$4,500.00 was paid outside of closing on May 5, 2025, directly by Borrower to Ashford Valuation Associates LLC. If shown on the settlement statement for disclosure purposes, this fee must be designated \"POC\" (Paid Outside of Closing) and must carry a \$0.00 value in any column that calculates Borrower\'s total charges or cash due at closing. Do not include this amount in Borrower\'s cash to close. The settlement agent is responsible for ensuring that this fee does not appear as a debit against the Borrower or as an amount to be collected at the closing table.

**6.3 Payoff and Lien Release Requirements**

The settlement agent must ensure that payoff funds for the following obligations are wire transferred on the closing date, with instructions to the respective lien holders for the prompt preparation and delivery of recordable releases or satisfactions:

> \(a\) Harborstone Federal Credit Union --- First Mortgage: \$687,412.33
>
> \(b\) Harborstone Federal Credit Union --- Home Equity Line of Credit: \$148,219.56
>
> \(c\) Northbridge Construction Co. --- Mechanic\'s Lien Settlement: \$31,000.00

The settlement agent must also obtain and record the release of the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services estate tax lien. Lender requires written confirmation from the settlement agent that all lien releases and satisfactions have been recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records within sixty (60) days following the closing date.

**6.4 Recording Instructions**

The following documents must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records in the following order of priority:

> 1\. Releases and satisfactions of all existing liens (Harborstone Federal Credit Union first mortgage, Harborstone Federal Credit Union HELOC, Northbridge Construction Co. mechanic\'s lien, and Connecticut DRS estate tax lien release);
>
> 2\. Warranty Deed from the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford (Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix) to Meridian Cove Properties LLC;
>
> 3\. Mortgage Deed from Meridian Cove Properties LLC to Tidewater Savings Bank;
>
> 4\. Assignment of Leases and Rents from Meridian Cove Properties LLC to Tidewater Savings Bank.

The settlement agent shall ensure that the recording sequence preserves Lender\'s first-priority lien position.

**6.5 Post-Closing Deliverables**

Within ten (10) business days following the closing date, the settlement agent shall deliver to Lender, attention Marcus J. Pellegrino, the following:

> \(a\) Recorded mortgage deed bearing the recording stamp of the Bridgeport Town Clerk;
>
> \(b\) Final marked-up title commitment or pro forma lender\'s title insurance policy;
>
> \(c\) Copies of all recorded documents, including the warranty deed, lien releases, and assignment of leases and rents;
>
> \(d\) Final settlement statement, signed by all parties to the transaction;
>
> \(e\) The original executed promissory note, to be held by Lender in its vault;
>
> \(f\) Copies of all executed lease assignments, estoppel certificates, and Subordination, Non-Disturbance, and Attornment Agreements (SNDAs).

**[Section 7: Commitment Expiration and General Terms]{.underline}**

**Expiration.** This Commitment shall expire at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on August 15, 2025, unless the Loan has closed prior to such date or this Commitment is extended in writing by Lender. Upon expiration, Lender shall have no further obligation to make the Loan.

**Commitment Fee.** No separate commitment fee is charged in connection with this Commitment. The loan origination fee described in Section 4.1 is payable at closing.

**Non-Assignability.** This Commitment is personal to Borrower and is not assignable by Borrower without the prior written consent of Lender, which consent may be granted or withheld in Lender\'s sole discretion.

**Governing Law.** This Commitment and all Loan documents to be executed in connection herewith shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Connecticut, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

**Material Adverse Change.** If, prior to the closing date, there is any material adverse change in Borrower\'s financial condition, the financial condition of the Guarantor, the physical condition or value of the Property, or the terms of the purchase transaction, Lender reserves the right to withdraw this Commitment upon written notice to Borrower.

**Expenses.** Borrower is responsible for all third-party costs and expenses incurred in connection with the Loan, whether or not the Loan closes, including but not limited to title insurance premiums, recording fees, appraisal fees (already paid as described herein), survey costs, environmental assessment costs, and attorneys\' fees and disbursements.

**Rate Lock.** The interest rate of 6.875% per annum is locked through the Commitment expiration date of August 15, 2025. If the Loan does not close on or before such date, the rate lock shall expire and Lender may quote a new interest rate based on then-prevailing market conditions.

**[Section 8: Acceptance]{.underline}**

To accept this Commitment, Borrower must sign and return a copy of this letter to the undersigned no later than July 1, 2025. Execution of this Commitment by Borrower constitutes Borrower\'s agreement to the terms and conditions set forth herein and in the Loan documents to be delivered at closing. Failure to return a signed copy of this Commitment by the acceptance deadline shall render this Commitment null and void at Lender\'s election.

We look forward to working with you to bring this transaction to a successful closing. Please do not hesitate to contact me with any questions or to coordinate the delivery of any required documentation.

TIDEWATER SAVINGS BANK

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Marcus J. Pellegrino

Title: Loan Officer, Commercial Real Estate Lending Division

Address: 3100 Post Road, Fairfield, CT 06824

Date: June 24, 2025

ACCEPTED AND AGREED:

MERIDIAN COVE PROPERTIES LLC, a Connecticut limited liability company

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Elaine R. Matsuda

Title: Manager

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

**EXHIBIT A**

**PROPERTY DESCRIPTION**

The property that is the subject of this Commitment Letter (the \"Property\") is described as follows:

**Legal Description:**

Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as shown on a map or plan entitled \"Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat,\" filed in the Office of the Bridgeport Town Clerk and recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records, City of Bridgeport, County of Fairfield, State of Connecticut.

**Street Address:** 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604

**Property Type:** Mixed-use commercial and residential

**Lot Size:** Approximately 0.38 acres (16,553 square feet)

**Total Building Area:** Approximately 18,400 square feet

**Year Built:** 1998; partial renovation completed in 2019

**Improvements:** Three-story mixed-use building comprising ground-floor commercial space (approximately 3,200 square feet) and twelve (12) residential apartment units on the second and third floors. Building is of masonry and steel frame construction with a flat roof membrane system.

**EXHIBIT B**

**SCHEDULE OF FEES AND COSTS**

The following schedule of loan-related fees and costs is provided for the benefit of the settlement agent, Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC, in preparing the settlement statement for the closing of Loan No. TSB-2025-CRE-04183.

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Fee**                             **Amount**              **Payable At / Status**
  ----------------------------------- ----------------------- ------------------------------
  Loan Origination Fee (1%)           \$26,400.00             At Closing

  Flood Certification Fee             \$25.00                 At Closing

  Tax Service Fee                     \$85.00                 At Closing

  Lender\'s Title Insurance Premium   \$3,850.00              At Closing

  Mortgage Recording Fee              \$113.00                At Closing

  Appraisal Fee                       \$4,500.00              **POC --- Paid May 5, 2025**

  **Total At Closing**                **\$30,473.00**         

  **Total Including POC**             **\$34,973.00**         
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**IMPORTANT NOTE TO SETTLEMENT AGENT:** The above schedule is provided for the benefit of the settlement agent in preparing the settlement statement. Only fees marked \"At Closing\" should be included in the Borrower\'s cash-to-close calculation. The appraisal fee of \$4,500.00 marked \"POC\" was collected directly by Lender\'s designated appraiser, Ashford Valuation Associates LLC, on May 5, 2025, prior to the closing, and has been paid in full by the Borrower outside of the closing transaction. This fee must not be reflected as a charge to Borrower at the closing table, must not be included in any computation of funds due from Borrower at closing, and must not appear as a debit in any column affecting Borrower\'s cash-to-close. If displayed on the settlement statement for informational or disclosure purposes, it must be designated \"POC --- Paid Outside of Closing\" with a \$0.00 impact.

This Commitment Letter and Closing Instructions, including Exhibits A and B, constitutes the entire commitment of Tidewater Savings Bank with respect to Loan No. TSB-2025-CRE-04183. This document contains confidential financial information and is intended solely for the use of the addressee and the professionals engaged in connection with this transaction.
