result:
**[PART A]{.underline}**

**[PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Between**

**ESTATE OF GERALD T. WHITFORD** **By and Through Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix** (\"Owner\")

**and**

**BAYSHORE MANAGEMENT CO.** (\"Manager\")

**Dated as of October 15, 2024**

**[ARTICLE 1 --- RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Gerald T. Whitford passed away on September 3, 2024, a resident of the City of Bridgeport, County of Fairfield, State of Connecticut;

**WHEREAS**, Claudia Whitford-Barnes was duly appointed as Executrix under the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford, which Will was admitted to probate in the Fairfield County Probate Court, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417, and Letters Testamentary were issued to Claudia Whitford-Barnes authorizing her to act on behalf of the Estate in all matters concerning the administration of the decedent\'s property and affairs;

**WHEREAS**, the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford is the owner of the real property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604, more particularly described as Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records (the \"Property\");

**WHEREAS**, the Property is a mixed-use building consisting of ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet currently leased to Coastal Provisions Market LLC, and twelve (12) residential apartment units designated as Units 2A through 2F on the second floor and Units 3A through 3F on the third floor; and

**WHEREAS**, Owner desires to engage Manager to manage the day-to-day operations of the Property during the estate administration period, and Manager desires to accept such engagement upon the terms and conditions set forth herein.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements herein contained, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE 2 --- APPOINTMENT AND AUTHORITY]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Appointment.** Owner hereby appoints Bayshore Management Co. as the exclusive property manager for the Property, and Manager hereby accepts such appointment, for the term and upon the conditions set forth in this Agreement. Manager\'s primary contact for all matters arising under this Agreement shall be Tomás Herrera, Operations Director.

**Section 2.2 --- Scope of Authority.** Manager is authorized and directed to collect all rents and other charges due from tenants of the Property, coordinate all maintenance and repair activities, enforce lease terms and conditions, and manage tenant relations on behalf of the Estate. Manager shall maintain all tenant records, including current rent rolls, security deposit accounts, and complete lease files for each residential and commercial tenant.

**Section 2.3 --- Compliance.** Manager shall perform all duties hereunder in accordance with all applicable Connecticut landlord-tenant laws, including without limitation Connecticut General Statutes Chapter 830 et seq., and all other applicable federal, state, and local laws, ordinances, and regulations.

**[ARTICLE 3 --- TERM]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Effective Date and Initial Term.** This Agreement shall be effective as of October 15, 2024 (the \"Effective Date\") and shall continue for an initial term of twelve (12) months, expiring on October 14, 2025 (the \"Initial Term\").

**Section 3.2 --- Renewal.** Following the expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive six (6) month periods unless terminated by either party in accordance with Article 11.

**Section 3.3 --- Early Termination.** Either party may terminate this Agreement upon not less than thirty (30) days\' prior written notice to the other party, subject to the provisions of Article 11.

**[ARTICLE 4 --- MANAGEMENT FEE]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Residential Management Fee.** Owner shall pay Manager a monthly management fee equal to six percent (6%) of gross collected residential rents for each calendar month during the term of this Agreement.

**Section 4.2 --- Commercial Management Fee.** In addition to the residential management fee, Owner shall pay Manager a flat fee of Three Hundred Fifty Dollars (\$350.00) per month for the management of the commercial lease with Coastal Provisions Market LLC.

**Section 4.3 --- Payment.** All management fees shall be payable on the first business day of each calendar month from the rent collections of the prior month. Manager is authorized to deduct its management fees directly from collected rents before remitting the net proceeds to Owner.

**[ARTICLE 5 --- RENT COLLECTION AND ACCOUNTING]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Collection.** Manager shall use commercially reasonable efforts to collect all rents and other charges due and payable under all residential and commercial leases affecting the Property. Manager shall promptly notify Owner of any tenant delinquencies exceeding fifteen (15) days past due.

**Section 5.2 --- Operating Account.** Manager shall maintain a separate operating account for the Property, segregated from Manager\'s own funds and from accounts maintained for other properties under Manager\'s management.

**Section 5.3 --- Monthly Statements.** Manager shall provide Owner with monthly accounting statements reflecting all income collected, expenses incurred, and management fees deducted, together with remittance of net proceeds. A copy of each monthly statement shall also be provided to Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP, Attention: David H. Clement, Esq., 200 Atlantic Street, 14th Floor, Stamford, Connecticut 06901, as counsel for the Estate.

**[ARTICLE 6 --- SECURITY DEPOSITS]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Residential Deposits.** Manager shall hold all residential security deposits in a separate, interest-bearing escrow account as required by applicable Connecticut law. As of the Effective Date, Manager holds residential security deposits for eleven (11) occupied units: Units 2A, 2B, 2C, 2D, 2E, 2F, 3A, 3B, 3D, 3E, and 3F. Unit 3C is currently vacant and no security deposit is on file for that unit.

**Section 6.2 --- Commercial Deposit.** Manager also holds the commercial security deposit of Fourteen Thousand Four Hundred Dollars (\$14,400.00) for Coastal Provisions Market LLC (Ryan and Sara Mulgrew, Principals) in a non-interest-bearing account, as the commercial lease for the ground-floor space does not require interest accrual on the security deposit.

**Section 6.3 --- Transfer Upon Termination.** Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, Manager shall transfer all security deposits, together with any accrued interest on residential deposits, to Owner or Owner\'s designee within five (5) business days of the effective date of termination.

**[ARTICLE 7 --- MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Ordinary Repairs.** Manager is authorized to make ordinary repairs and perform routine maintenance on the Property in amounts not exceeding Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$2,500.00) per occurrence without prior written approval from Owner.

**Section 7.2 --- Major Repairs.** Any expenditure exceeding Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$2,500.00) per occurrence shall require the prior written authorization of Owner or Owner\'s counsel before Manager may commit to or incur such expense.

**Section 7.3 --- Emergency Repairs.** Notwithstanding Section 7.2, Manager may authorize emergency repairs necessary to protect life, health, or property without limitation as to amount, provided that Manager shall give prompt notice to Owner of any such emergency expenditure.

**[ARTICLE 8 --- INSURANCE]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Manager\'s Insurance.** Manager shall obtain and maintain, at Manager\'s sole cost and expense, commercial general liability insurance with coverage of not less than One Million Dollars (\$1,000,000.00) per occurrence, naming the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford as an additional insured. Certificates of insurance shall be provided to Owner upon request.

**Section 8.2 --- Owner\'s Insurance.** Owner shall maintain, at Owner\'s sole cost and expense, property insurance and landlord liability coverage for the Property in amounts deemed adequate by Owner and Owner\'s counsel.

**[ARTICLE 9 --- INDEMNIFICATION]{.underline}**

**Section 9.1 --- Indemnification by Manager.** Manager shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Owner, the Estate, and their respective agents, attorneys, and representatives from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or resulting from Manager\'s negligence, willful misconduct, or breach of this Agreement.

**Section 9.2 --- Indemnification by Owner.** Owner shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Manager and its officers, employees, and agents from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or resulting from conditions of the Property or actions taken by Owner that are not caused by or attributable to the acts or omissions of Manager.

**[ARTICLE 10 --- REPRESENTATIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 10.1 --- Manager\'s Representations.** Manager represents and warrants that it is a business entity duly organized and in good standing under the laws of the State of Connecticut, and that it holds all licenses, permits, and certifications required for the conduct of property management services in the State of Connecticut.

**Section 10.2 --- Owner\'s Representations.** Owner represents and warrants that Claudia Whitford-Barnes has been duly appointed as Executrix of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford and possesses full legal authority to enter into this Agreement and to bind the Estate to the obligations contained herein.

**[ARTICLE 11 --- TERMINATION]{.underline}**

**Section 11.1 --- Termination for Convenience.** Either party may terminate this Agreement upon not less than thirty (30) days\' prior written notice to the other party, delivered in accordance with Article 12. Such termination shall be effective as of the date specified in the notice, provided that such date is not fewer than thirty (30) days following the date of delivery of the notice.

**Section 11.2 --- Termination Fee.** In the event of termination by Owner prior to the expiration of the Initial Term for any reason other than Manager\'s material breach of this Agreement, Owner shall pay Manager a termination fee of Four Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$4,500.00), which fee shall be due and payable on the effective date of termination. If termination occurs in connection with the sale of the Property, the termination fee of Four Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$4,500.00) shall be payable from the proceeds of sale at closing and may be included as a charge on the settlement statement for the transaction.

**Section 11.3 --- Transition Obligations.** Upon the effective date of termination, Manager shall: (a) deliver all books, records, lease files, tenant correspondence, keys, and access devices to Owner or Owner\'s designee; (b) transfer all security deposits, together with any accrued interest on residential deposits, to Owner or Owner\'s designee in accordance with Article 6; (c) provide a final accounting of all income, expenses, and deposits within fifteen (15) days after the effective date of termination; and (d) cooperate fully with Owner or any successor property manager in the orderly transition of all management responsibilities for the Property.

**[ARTICLE 12 --- NOTICES]{.underline}**

All notices, demands, requests, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when delivered personally, when sent by certified mail (return receipt requested, postage prepaid), or when sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service, addressed as follows:

**If to Owner:**

> Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix Estate of Gerald T. Whitford c/o Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP 200 Atlantic Street, 14th Floor Stamford, Connecticut 06901
>
> Attention: David H. Clement, Esq.

**If to Manager:**

> Bayshore Management Co.
>
> Attention: Tomás Herrera, Operations Director
>
> 54 Seaside Avenue, Suite 200 Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604

Either party may change its address for notice purposes by providing written notice to the other party in accordance with this Article 12.

**[ARTICLE 13 --- MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**Section 13.1 --- Governing Law.** This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Connecticut, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

**Section 13.2 --- Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, and agreements, whether written or oral. No amendment, modification, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless set forth in a writing signed by both parties.

**Section 13.3 --- Assignment.** Neither party may assign this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.

**Section 13.4 --- Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

**Section 13.5 --- Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the parties have executed this Property Management Agreement as of the date first written above.

**OWNER:**

ESTATE OF GERALD T. WHITFORD

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Claudia Whitford-Barnes

Title: Executrix

Date: October 15, 2024

**MANAGER:**

BAYSHORE MANAGEMENT CO.

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Tomás Herrera

Title: Operations Director

Date: October 15, 2024

**[PART B]{.underline}**

**[NOTICE OF TERMINATION OF PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

June 13, 2025

**VIA CERTIFIED MAIL, RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED**

Bayshore Management Co.

Attention: Tomás Herrera, Operations Director

54 Seaside Avenue, Suite 200 Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604

> **Re: Notice of Termination --- Property Management Agreement dated October 15, 2024, concerning 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604**

Dear Mr. Herrera:

I write on behalf of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, in my capacity as Executrix, to provide formal notice of termination of the Property Management Agreement dated October 15, 2024 (the \"Agreement\") between the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford (\"Owner\") and Bayshore Management Co. (\"Manager\") concerning the property located at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604 (the \"Property\").

Pursuant to Section 11.1 of the Agreement, Owner hereby exercises its right to terminate the Agreement, effective July 15, 2025. This termination is being made in connection with the sale of the Property to Meridian Cove Properties LLC, with closing scheduled for July 15, 2025.

Owner acknowledges that, because this termination occurs prior to the expiration of the Initial Term and is not the result of Manager\'s material breach, the termination fee of Four Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$4,500.00) is due and payable under Section 11.2 of the Agreement. In accordance with Section 11.2, the termination fee shall be payable from the proceeds of sale at closing and will be reflected as a charge on the settlement statement for the transaction.

Owner respectfully requests that Manager comply with all transition obligations set forth in Section 11.3 of the Agreement, including the following:

1\. **Delivery of Records and Keys.** On or before the closing date of July 15, 2025, Manager shall deliver all books, records, lease files, tenant correspondence, keys, access codes, and access devices pertaining to the Property to the buyer, Meridian Cove Properties LLC, or buyer\'s designee.

2\. **Transfer of Security Deposits.** Manager shall transfer all security deposits --- including all residential security deposits with accrued interest, and the commercial security deposit of \$14,400.00 for Coastal Provisions Market LLC (Ryan and Sara Mulgrew, Principals) --- to the closing escrow agent, Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC, Attention: Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer, 88 Field Point Road, Greenwich, Connecticut 06830, or as otherwise directed by the parties at closing.

3\. **Final Accounting.** Manager shall provide a final accounting of all income collected, expenses incurred, management fees deducted, and deposit balances held, to be delivered within fifteen (15) days of July 15, 2025.

Manager is directed to continue normal management operations for the Property through July 14, 2025, and to cooperate fully with representatives of the buyer, Meridian Cove Properties LLC (managed by Elaine R. Matsuda), and buyer\'s counsel, Nora F. Kapadia, Esq., Ridgeline Law Group PLLC, 915 Chapel Street, Suite 400, New Haven, Connecticut 06510, with respect to any pre-closing inspections, tenant communications, or informational requests. Buyer\'s counsel may contact you directly regarding current unit status, outstanding maintenance issues, and final rent collection for July 2025.

On behalf of the Estate, I wish to express our sincere appreciation for Bayshore Management Co.\'s diligent and professional service in managing the Property during the estate administration period. Your attention to the tenants and to the upkeep of the building has been of great value to the Estate during a difficult time.

Please do not hesitate to contact me or David H. Clement, Esq. at Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP if you have any questions regarding this notice or the closing process.

Sincerely,

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix Estate of Gerald T. Whitford

**[ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF RECEIPT]{.underline}**

The undersigned acknowledges receipt of the foregoing Notice of Termination and agrees to comply with the transition obligations set forth in Section 11.3 of the Property Management Agreement dated October 15, 2024.

BAYSHORE MANAGEMENT CO.

result:
**[STATE OF CONNECTICUT --- PROBATE COURT]{.underline}**

**[FAIRFIELD COUNTY PROBATE COURT]{.underline}**

Fairfield County Courthouse Bridgeport, Connecticut 06604

**FIDUCIARY\'S CERTIFICATE**

**Docket No.: 2024-PR-04417**

**Estate of: Gerald T. Whitford, Deceased**

**[Section 1 --- Certificate of Appointment]{.underline}**

The Fairfield County Probate Court, a court of competent jurisdiction duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Connecticut, pursuant to Connecticut General Statutes § 45a-234 et seq., hereby certifies as follows:

**Claudia Whitford-Barnes**, an individual residing at 47 Winding Brook Lane, Stamford, Connecticut 06902, was duly appointed as **Executrix** of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, deceased, late of Fairfield County, Connecticut. Gerald T. Whitford died on **September 3, 2024**, a resident of Fairfield County, Connecticut.

The Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford was admitted to probate in this Court on or about **October 11, 2024**, and Letters Testamentary were issued to Claudia Whitford-Barnes on that same date, authorizing her to act as Executrix of the decedent\'s estate with all powers and duties prescribed by law and by the terms of said Last Will and Testament.

A probate bond in the amount of **Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$500,000.00)** was set by this Court, and said bond has been duly filed with the Court and approved in accordance with applicable statutory requirements.

As of the date of this certificate, the Executrix is currently serving in her fiduciary capacity and has not been removed, has not resigned, and has not had her authority revoked, suspended, or otherwise limited by order of this Court or by operation of law.

**[Section 2 --- Scope of Fiduciary Authority]{.underline}**

This Court further certifies that, under the terms of the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford and applicable provisions of the Connecticut General Statutes, the Executrix is authorized to:

> \(a\) Manage, control, sell, convey, lease, mortgage, exchange, or otherwise dispose of any and all real and personal property belonging to the estate, at such prices and upon such terms and conditions as the Executrix, in the exercise of her fiduciary judgment, deems to be in the best interests of the estate and its beneficiaries;
>
> \(b\) Execute deeds, contracts of sale, purchase and sale agreements, assignments, bills of sale, and all other instruments and documents necessary or appropriate to effectuate such transactions;
>
> \(c\) Retain counsel, engage real estate professionals, appraisers, accountants, property managers, and other professionals as reasonably necessary for the proper administration of the estate and the disposition of estate assets.

The Executrix is specifically authorized to sell and convey the real property known as **4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604**, more particularly described as **Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records**, without further order of this Court.

The Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford contains an independent administration provision granting the Executrix broad discretionary authority over estate assets. Accordingly, no additional court approval, order, or authorization is required for the sale, conveyance, or transfer of the above-described real property or any other estate asset.

**[Section 3 --- Certification and Authentication]{.underline}**

This certificate is issued at the request of the fiduciary for the purpose of confirming her authority to convey real property belonging to the estate. This certificate is valid as of the date issued below and may be relied upon by purchasers, title insurance companies, mortgage lenders, closing agents, and their respective counsel as evidence of the Executrix\'s appointment and authority.

This Fiduciary\'s Certificate is dated **June 25, 2025**.

Hon. Margaret A. Faircloth Judge of Probate Fairfield County Probate Court

\[SEAL OF THE FAIRFIELD COUNTY PROBATE COURT\]

Attest: Patricia R. Dunleavy Clerk of the Probate Court Fairfield County Probate Court

I hereby certify that the foregoing is a true and correct copy of the Fiduciary\'s Certificate on file in this Court in the matter of the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417. Certification fee of **\$150.00** has been paid.

--- Patricia R. Dunleavy, Clerk of the Probate Court

**ASHFORD, CLEMENT & PAIGE LLP** Counselors at Law 200 Atlantic Street, 14th Floor Stamford, Connecticut 06901 Telephone: (203) 555-4100 Facsimile: (203) 555-4101

**ESTATE SUMMARY --- PREPARED IN CONNECTION WITH REAL PROPERTY CONVEYANCE**

July 10, 2025

**Prepared by:** David H. Clement, Esq.

**Re:** Estate of Gerald T. Whitford, Docket No. 2024-PR-04417; Sale of 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604

**Addressed to:**

> Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC
>
> Attn: Lorraine M. Grasso, Closing Officer
>
> Ridgeline Law Group PLLC
>
> Attn: Nora F. Kapadia, Esq.

**Section A --- Decedent and Estate Overview**

Gerald T. Whitford died on **September 3, 2024**, and was at the time of his death a resident of Fairfield County, Connecticut. Mr. Whitford\'s Last Will and Testament, dated March 14, 2019, was admitted to probate in the Fairfield County Probate Court under Docket No. **2024-PR-04417**. Letters Testamentary were issued to **Claudia Whitford-Barnes** on October 11, 2024, appointing her as Executrix of the estate. Ms. Whitford-Barnes is the decedent\'s daughter.

The estate is represented by this firm, Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP, with the undersigned, David H. Clement, Esq., serving as attorney of record.

The real property located at **4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604** constitutes the principal real estate asset of the estate. The property has been in the Whitford family since **2001**, when Gerald T. Whitford originally purchased it. The Executrix has determined, in the exercise of her fiduciary judgment and following appropriate appraisal, that the sale of this property is in the best interests of the estate and its beneficiaries.

**Section B --- Property Description**

**Address:** 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604

**Legal Description:** Lot 17, Block 42, Bridgeport Harbor Redevelopment Plat, as recorded in Volume 312, Page 88 of the Bridgeport Land Records.

**Property Type:** Mixed-use. The property consists of a ground-floor commercial space of approximately 3,200 square feet with twelve (12) residential apartment units located on the second and third floors, designated as Units 2A through 2F and Units 3A through 3F.

**Year Built:** 1998, with a partial renovation completed in 2019.

**Zoning:** MU-2 (Mixed-Use Transit-Oriented District).

The property is subject to existing leases, including a commercial lease with **Coastal Provisions Market LLC**, the ground-floor tenant, and various residential tenancy agreements covering the upper-floor apartments. Management of the residential units has been handled by **Bayshore Management Co.** (Operations Director: Tomás Herrera) since October 2024, when the Executrix engaged the management company following the decedent\'s passing.

**Section C --- Transaction Summary**

The Executrix has entered into a Purchase and Sale Agreement dated **April 22, 2025** with **Meridian Cove Properties LLC**, a Connecticut limited liability company, for the sale of the property at a purchase price of **Three Million Nine Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$3,900,000.00)**.

The anticipated closing date is **July 15, 2025**. The Buyer is represented by **Nora F. Kapadia, Esq.** of **Ridgeline Law Group PLLC**. The closing is to be conducted by **Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC**, with Lorraine M. Grasso serving as Closing Officer. The Buyer\'s acquisition is being financed in part by **Tidewater Savings Bank** (Loan Officer: Marcus J. Pellegrino).

**Section D --- Existing Encumbrances and Liens**

The following is a summary of known liens and encumbrances of record against the property, all of which are to be satisfied, released, or otherwise resolved at or before closing:

> **1. First Mortgage --- Harborstone Federal Credit Union.** A mortgage originated on March 15, 2015, in the original principal amount of \$1,200,000.00, secured by the subject property. A payoff statement will be obtained from Harborstone Federal Credit Union, and this mortgage shall be satisfied in full at closing from the sale proceeds.
>
> **2. Home Equity Line of Credit --- Harborstone Federal Credit Union.** A home equity line of credit with a maximum credit limit of \$300,000.00, also secured by the subject property. A payoff statement will be obtained from Harborstone Federal Credit Union, and this line of credit shall be satisfied in full and closed at closing from the sale proceeds.
>
> **3. Mechanic\'s Lien --- Northbridge Construction Co.** A mechanic\'s lien was filed on August 12, 2024, in the amount of \$37,500.00, arising from a dispute regarding unpaid work performed in connection with the 2019 renovation of the property. A settlement has been reached between the estate and Northbridge Construction Co. for a reduced payoff amount of \$31,000.00, which shall be paid at closing. Upon receipt of payment, Northbridge Construction Co. will execute and deliver a release of lien for recording.
>
> **4. Connecticut Department of Revenue Services (CT DRS) --- Estate Tax Lien.** A precautionary estate tax lien was filed by the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services against the subject property in connection with the decedent\'s estate. The estate\'s gross taxable estate, as determined and reported on the applicable Connecticut Estate Tax Return, is below the Connecticut estate tax exemption threshold for decedents dying in 2024. Accordingly, the Connecticut estate tax obligation attributable to the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford is **\$0.00**. No payment is due to CT DRS. The lien was filed as a routine precautionary measure by the Department and does not reflect any outstanding tax obligation. A release of the estate tax lien has been obtained from CT DRS and will be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records at closing to clear the lien of record. The release instrument must be recorded to satisfy title requirements, but no payoff or monetary satisfaction of any kind is required in connection with this lien.

**Section E --- Estate Tax Status --- Detailed Notation**

Under Connecticut General Statutes § 12-391 et seq., the Connecticut estate tax applies to estates with a Connecticut taxable estate exceeding the applicable exemption amount. The total gross estate of Gerald T. Whitford, as reported on the Connecticut Estate Tax Return (Form CT-706/709) filed with the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services, is below the applicable Connecticut estate tax exemption threshold for decedents dying in calendar year 2024.

**Connecticut estate tax due from the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford: \$0.00.**

The CT DRS has reviewed the estate\'s return, confirmed that no estate tax is owing, and has issued a release of the estate tax lien, designated as **CT DRS Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834**. This release must be recorded in the Bridgeport Land Records to clear the precautionary lien of record and satisfy the title requirements of the purchaser and its title insurer. Recording of the release is a title requirement but carries no associated payoff obligation. No funds are to be disbursed to the Connecticut Department of Revenue Services at closing.

For completeness, this firm confirms that the estate\'s federal estate tax status is not directly relevant to the title clearing for this transaction. Appropriate federal filings have been or will be timely made in accordance with applicable Internal Revenue Code requirements.

**Section F --- Representations and Enclosures**

The Executrix represents that she has full authority to sell and convey the subject property, that the estate is not subject to any pending litigation that would impair the conveyance, other than the mechanic\'s lien noted in Section D(3) above, which is being resolved by agreed settlement at closing, and that there are no other claims, disputes, or proceedings of which the Executrix is aware that would affect marketable title to the property.

The Executrix further confirms that all required probate court notices have been given in accordance with Connecticut General Statutes and applicable court rules, and that no objections to the sale of the property have been filed with the Fairfield County Probate Court.

The following documents are enclosed with this Estate Summary:

> 1\. Certified copy of Fiduciary Certificate (dated June 25, 2025)
>
> 2\. Certified copy of Letters Testamentary (issued October 11, 2024)
>
> 3\. Copy of relevant pages of the Last Will and Testament of Gerald T. Whitford (provisions authorizing sale of real property)
>
> 4\. CT DRS Estate Tax Lien Release (Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834)
>
> 5\. CT DRS letter confirming \$0.00 estate tax obligation

All inquiries regarding this Estate Summary or the estate\'s interests in the transaction should be directed to the undersigned at the contact information provided below.

Respectfully submitted,

ASHFORD, CLEMENT & PAIGE LLP

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

David H. Clement, Esq. 200 Atlantic Street, 14th Floor Stamford, Connecticut 06901 Telephone: (203) 555-4100

Email: dclement@ashfordclement.com

Counsel for the Estate of Gerald T. Whitford

Acknowledged and Confirmed:

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix Estate of Gerald T. Whitford Docket No. 2024-PR-04417

# Verify all settlement statement calculations

print("=" * 70)
print("SETTLEMENT STATEMENT CALCULATION VERIFICATION")
print("=" * 70)

# === PURCHASE PRICE & FINANCING ===
purchase_price = 3900000.00
loan_amount = 2640000.00
earnest_money = 195000.00  # 100k + 95k

# === PRORATION 1: REAL PROPERTY TAX ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 1: REAL PROPERTY TAX ---")
annual_tax = 52480.00
per_diem = annual_tax / 365
print(f"Annual tax (FY2025-26 estimate): ${annual_tax:,.2f}")
print(f"Per diem (365-day year): ${per_diem:.6f}/day")

seller_tax_days = 14  # July 1 - July 14
buyer_tax_days = 351  # July 15 - June 30
seller_tax_share = round(per_diem * seller_tax_days, 2)
buyer_tax_share = round(per_diem * buyer_tax_days, 2)
print(f"Seller share (14 days): ${seller_tax_share:,.2f}")
print(f"Buyer share (351 days): ${buyer_tax_share:,.2f}")
print(f"Check sum: ${seller_tax_share + buyer_tax_share:,.2f} (should be ${annual_tax:,.2f})")

# Delinquent tax
delinquent_principal = 26240.00
delinquent_interest = 2362.80
delinquent_total = delinquent_principal + delinquent_interest
print(f"\nDelinquent 2nd installment: ${delinquent_principal:,.2f}")
print(f"Interest through 7/15/2025: ${delinquent_interest:,.2f}")
print(f"Total delinquent: ${delinquent_total:,.2f}")

# === PRORATION 2: RENTS (COLLECTED) ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 2: RENTS (COLLECTED, July 2025, 31 days) ---")
print("Seller: 14 days (7/1-7/14), Buyer: 17 days (7/15-7/31)")
leases = [
    ("GF Commercial", 7200.00, "Paid"),
    ("2A", 1650.00, "Paid"),
    ("2B", 1575.00, "Paid"),
    ("2C", 1700.00, "Paid"),
    ("2D", 1525.00, "Paid"),
    ("2E", 1600.00, "UNPAID"),
    ("2F", 1650.00, "Paid"),
    ("3A", 1750.00, "Paid"),
    ("3B", 1575.00, "Paid"),
    ("3C", 0.00, "VACANT"),
    ("3D", 1700.00, "Paid"),
    ("3E", 1625.00, "Paid"),
    ("3F", 1650.00, "Paid"),
]

total_collected = 0
seller_rent_total = 0
buyer_rent_total = 0
print(f"{'Unit':<16}{'Monthly':>10}{'Status':<10}{'Seller(14/31)':>14}{'Buyer(17/31)':>14}")
for unit, rent, status in leases:
    if status == "Paid":
        s = round(rent * 14 / 31, 2)
        b = round(rent - s, 2)
        total_collected += rent
        seller_rent_total += s
        buyer_rent_total += b
        print(f"{unit:<16}{rent:>10,.2f}{status:<10}{s:>14,.2f}{b:>14,.2f}")
    else:
        print(f"{unit:<16}{rent:>10,.2f}{status:<10}{'0.00 (excl)':>14}{'0.00 (excl)':>14}")

print(f"{'TOTAL':<16}{total_collected:>10,.2f}{'':<10}{seller_rent_total:>14,.2f}{buyer_rent_total:>14,.2f}")
print(f"Check: Seller + Buyer = ${seller_rent_total + buyer_rent_total:,.2f} (should be ${total_collected:,.2f})")
print(f"Net rent proration: Debit Seller / Credit Buyer = ${buyer_rent_total:,.2f}")

# Aggregate method comparison
agg_seller = round(total_collected * 14 / 31, 2)
agg_buyer = round(total_collected * 17 / 31, 2)
print(f"\nAggregate method comparison: Seller=${agg_seller:,.2f}, Buyer=${agg_buyer:,.2f}")
print(f"Per-lease method: Seller=${seller_rent_total:,.2f}, Buyer=${buyer_rent_total:,.2f}")
print(f"Difference (per-lease buyer - agg buyer): ${buyer_rent_total - agg_buyer:,.2f}")

# === PRORATION 3: SECURITY DEPOSITS ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 3: SECURITY DEPOSITS ---")
deposits = [
    ("GF Commercial", 14400.00),
    ("2A", 1650.00), ("2B", 1575.00), ("2C", 1700.00), ("2D", 1525.00),
    ("2E", 1600.00), ("2F", 1650.00), ("3A", 1750.00), ("3B", 1575.00),
    ("3D", 1700.00), ("3E", 1625.00), ("3F", 1650.00),
]
total_deposits = sum(d for _, d in deposits)
print(f"Total security deposits (principal): ${total_deposits:,.2f}")
res_deposits = sum(d for u, d in deposits if u != "GF Commercial")
comm_deposits = 14400.00
print(f"  Residential (11 units): ${res_deposits:,.2f}")
print(f"  Commercial (1 unit): ${comm_deposits:,.2f}")

# === PRORATION 4: UTILITIES ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 4: UTILITIES (WATER/SEWER) ---")
utility = 1847.60
print(f"Billing period: May 15 - July 14, 2025 (ends BEFORE closing 7/15)")
print(f"Total charges: ${utility:,.2f} (UNPAID, due 8/1/2025)")
print(f"Per PSA 7.5(a): Entire bill = Seller responsibility, credited to Buyer")
print(f"Debit Seller / Credit Buyer = ${utility:,.2f}")

# === PRORATION 5: HEATING OIL ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 5: HEATING OIL ---")
gallons = 180
price = 3.85
oil_value = gallons * price
print(f"Tank reading (7/14/2025): {gallons} gallons")
print(f"Price/gallon (last delivery 6/2/2025): ${price}")
print(f"Value: {gallons} x ${price} = ${oil_value:,.2f}")
print(f"Credit Seller / Debit Buyer = ${oil_value:,.2f}")

# === LIEN PAYOFFS ===
print("\n--- LIEN PAYOFFS (Seller, from proceeds) ---")
first_mortgage = 687412.33
heloc = 148219.56
mechanics_lien = 31000.00
total_liens = first_mortgage + heloc + mechanics_lien
print(f"Harborstone 1st Mortgage: ${first_mortgage:,.2f}")
print(f"Harborstone HELOC: ${heloc:,.2f}")
print(f"Northbridge Mechanic's Lien: ${mechanics_lien:,.2f}")
print(f"CT DRS Estate Tax Lien: $0.00 (release only)")
print(f"Total lien payoffs: ${total_liens:,.2f}")

# Verify first mortgage payoff components
print(f"\n  1st Mortgage breakdown: UPB ${685921.44:,.2f} + Int ${876.89:,.2f} + Escrow ${539.00:,.2f} + Release prep ${75.00:,.2f} = ${685921.44+876.89+539.00+75.00:,.2f}")
# Verify HELOC payoff components
print(f"  HELOC breakdown: Principal ${147875.58:,.2f} + Int ${343.98:,.2f} = ${147875.58+343.98:,.2f}")

EOF"}

deposits = [
    ("GF Commercial", 14400.00),
    ("2A", 1650.00), ("2B", 1575.00), ("2C", 1700.00), ("2D", 1525.00),
    ("2E", 1600.00), ("2F", 1650.00), ("3A", 1750.00), ("3B", 1575.00),
    ("3D", 1700.00), ("3E", 1625.00), ("3F", 1650.00),
]
total_deposits = sum(d for _, d in deposits)
print(f"Total security deposits (principal): ${total_deposits:,.2f}")
res_deposits = sum(d for u, d in deposits if u != "GF Commercial")
comm_deposits = 14400.00
print(f"  Residential (11 units): ${res_deposits:,.2f}")
print(f"  Commercial (1 unit): ${comm_deposits:,.2f}")

# === PRORATION 4: UTILITIES ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 4: UTILITIES (WATER/SEWER) ---")
utility = 1847.60
print(f"Billing period: May 15 - July 14, 2025 (ends BEFORE closing 7/15)")
print(f"Total charges: ${utility:,.2f} (UNPAID, due 8/1/2025)")
print(f"Per PSA 7.5(a): Entire bill = Seller responsibility, credited to Buyer")
print(f"Debit Seller / Credit Buyer = ${utility:,.2f}")

# === PRORATION 5: HEATING OIL ===
print("\n--- PRORATION 5: HEATING OIL ---")
gallons = 180
price = 3.85
oil_value = gallons * price
print(f"Tank reading (7/14/2025): {gallons} gallons")
print(f"Price/gallon (last delivery 6/2/2025): ${price}")
print(f"Value: {gallons} x ${price} = ${oil_value:,.2f}")
print(f"Credit Seller / Debit Buyer = ${oil_value:,.2f}")

# === LIEN PAYOFFS ===
print("\n--- LIEN PAYOFFS (Seller, from proceeds) ---")
first_mortgage = 687412.33
heloc = 148219.56
mechanics_lien = 31000.00
total_liens = first_mortgage + heloc + mechanics_lien
print(f"Harborstone 1st Mortgage: ${first_mortgage:,.2f}")
print(f"Harborstone HELOC: ${heloc:,.2f}")
print(f"Northbridge Mechanic's Lien: ${mechanics_lien:,.2f}")
print(f"CT DRS Estate Tax Lien: $0.00 (release only)")
print(f"Total lien payoffs: ${total_liens:,.2f}")

# Verify first mortgage payoff components
print(f"\n  1st Mortgage breakdown: UPB ${685921.44:,.2f} + Int ${876.89:,.2f} + Escrow ${539.00:,.2f} + Release prep ${75.00:,.2f} = ${685921.44+876.89+539.00+75.00:,.2f}")
# Verify HELOC payoff components
print(f"  HELOC breakdown: Principal ${147875.58:,.2f} + Int ${343.98:,.2f} = ${147875.58+343.98:,.2f}")

EOF"}

# Debug Unit 2D rent proration
rent = 1525.00
seller_raw = rent * 14 / 31
buyer_raw = rent * 17 / 31
print(f"2D rent: {rent}")
print(f"Seller raw: {rent} * 14 / 31 = {seller_raw!r}")
print(f"Seller rounded: {round(seller_raw, 2)}")
print(f"Buyer raw: {rent} * 17 / 31 = {buyer_raw!r}")
print(f"Buyer rounded: {round(buyer_raw, 2)}")
print(f"Buyer = rent - seller_rounded: {rent - round(seller_raw, 2)}")
print()

# Check all leases with full precision
leases = [
    ("GF Commercial", 7200.00), ("2A", 1650.00), ("2B", 1575.00),
    ("2C", 1700.00), ("2D", 1525.00), ("2F", 1650.00), ("3A", 1750.00),
    ("3B", 1575.00), ("3D", 1700.00), ("3E", 1625.00), ("3F", 1650.00),
]
seller_total = 0
buyer_total = 0
for unit, r in leases:
    s = round(r * 14 / 31, 2)
    b = round(r - s, 2)

    buyer_total += b

print(f"Buyer total:  {buyer_total:,.2f}")
print(f"Sum:          {seller_total+buyer_total:,.2f}")
EOF"}

print(f"509. Tax Proration (Seller share):${tax_proration:>14,.2f}")

print(f"511. Seller Recording Fees:       ${292.00:>14,.2f}")
print(f"512. CT Conveyance Tax (50%):     ${22375.00:>14,.2f}")
print(f"513. Seller Attorney Fees:        ${11000.00:>14,.2f}")
print(f"514. Probate Court Certificate:   ${150.00:>14,.2f}")
print(f"515. Mgmt Agreement Termination:  ${4500.00:>14,.2f}")
print(f"516. Repair Escrow (Holdback):    ${repair_escrow:>14,.2f}")
print(f"530. TOTAL REDUCTIONS:            ${reductions:>14,.2f}")

cash_to_seller = gross_due_to_seller - reductions
print(f"\n600. Gross Amount Due to Seller:  ${gross_due_to_seller:>14,.2f}")
print(f"601. Less Total Reductions:       ${reductions:>14,.2f}")
print(f"602. CASH TO SELLER:              ${cash_to_seller:>14,.2f}")

# === RECONCILIATION ===
print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("RECONCILIATION")
print("=" * 70)
funds_in = cash_from_buyer + loan + earnest
print(f"\nFUNDS RECEIVED:")
print(f"  Cash from Buyer:                ${cash_from_buyer:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Loan Proceeds:                  ${loan:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Earnest Money (held):           ${earnest:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  TOTAL FUNDS IN:                 ${funds_in:>14,.2f}")

funds_out = (cash_to_seller + liens + delinquent_tax + repair_escrow +
    buyer_settlement_charges + seller_settlement_charges)
print(f"\nFUNDS DISBURSED:")
print(f"  Net Proceeds to Seller:         ${cash_to_seller:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Lien Payoffs:                   ${liens:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Delinquent Tax Payoff:          ${delinquent_tax:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Repair Escrow (segregated):     ${repair_escrow:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Buyer Settlement Charges:       ${buyer_settlement_charges:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Seller Settlement Charges:      ${seller_settlement_charges:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  TOTAL FUNDS OUT:                ${funds_out:>14,.2f}")

print(f"\nDIFFERENCE (in - out):           ${funds_in - funds_out:>14,.2f}")
print("RECONCILED" if abs(funds_in - funds_out) < 0.01 else "MISMATCH!")

# Equity check
total_equity = earnest + cash_from_buyer
print(f"\n--- EQUITY CHECK ---")
print(f"Total equity (earnest + cash): ${total_equity:,.2f}")
print(f"Lender minimum equity req:     $1,260,000.00")
print(f"Meets requirement: {total_equity >= 1260000}")
EOF"}

print(f"\n600. Gross Amount Due to Seller:  ${gross_due_to_seller:>14,.2f}")
print(f"601. Less Total Reductions:       ${reductions:>14,.2f}")
print(f"602. CASH TO SELLER:              ${cash_to_seller:>14,.2f}")

# === RECONCILIATION ===
print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("RECONCILIATION")
print("=" * 70)
funds_in = cash_from_buyer + loan + earnest
print(f"\nFUNDS RECEIVED:")
print(f"  Cash from Buyer:                ${cash_from_buyer:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Loan Proceeds:                  ${loan:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Earnest Money (held):           ${earnest:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  TOTAL FUNDS IN:                 ${funds_in:>14,.2f}")

funds_out = (cash_to_seller + liens + delinquent_tax + repair_escrow +
    buyer_settlement_charges + seller_settlement_charges)
print(f"\nFUNDS DISBURSED:")
print(f"  Net Proceeds to Seller:         ${cash_to_seller:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Lien Payoffs:                   ${liens:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Delinquent Tax Payoff:          ${delinquent_tax:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Repair Escrow (segregated):     ${repair_escrow:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Buyer Settlement Charges:       ${buyer_settlement_charges:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  Seller Settlement Charges:      ${seller_settlement_charges:>14,.2f}")
print(f"  TOTAL FUNDS OUT:                ${funds_out:>14,.2f}")

print(f"\nDIFFERENCE (in - out):           ${funds_in - funds_out:>14,.2f}")
print("RECONCILED" if abs(funds_in - funds_out) < 0.01 else "MISMATCH!")

# Equity check
total_equity = earnest + cash_from_buyer
print(f"\n--- EQUITY CHECK ---")
print(f"Total equity (earnest + cash): ${total_equity:,.2f}")
print(f"Lender minimum equity req:     $1,260,000.00")
print(f"Meets requirement: {total_equity >= 1260000}")
EOF"}

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:

result:
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

## DETAILED PRORATION SCHEDULES

### Schedule A — Real Property Tax Proration

**A-1. Current Fiscal Year (July 1, 2024 – June 30, 2025) — Delinquent Amount (Seller's Sole Responsibility)**

| Component | Amount |
|---|---:|
| Annual tax levy (FY 2024–2025) | $52,480.00 |
| 1st Installment ($26,240.00, due 7/1/2024) — PAID 8/1/2024 | $0.00 (paid) |
| 2nd Installment ($26,240.00, due 1/1/2025) — DELINQUENT | $26,240.00 |
| Accrued interest through 7/15/2025 (CT § 12-146, 1.5%/mo. from 1/2/2025) | $2,362.80 |
| **Total Delinquent Amount (paid from Seller's proceeds, Line 504 + 505)** | **$28,602.80** |

> This is not a proration. The delinquent 2nd installment and all accrued interest/penalties are Seller's sole responsibility and are paid in full from Seller's proceeds at closing. Per diem interest after 7/15/2025 accrues at $13.12/day; payoff valid through 7/20/2025.

**A-2. Next Fiscal Year (July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026) — Estimated Proration**

| Component | Value |
|---|---:|
| Estimated annual tax (using current FY amount per Sec. 7.1(b)) | $52,480.00 |
| Per diem ($52,480.00 ÷ 365-day year) | $143.7808/day |
| Seller's period: July 1 – July 14, 2025 (14 days) | 14 days |
| Seller's share: 14 × $143.7808 | **$2,012.93** |
| Buyer's period: July 15, 2025 – June 30, 2026 (351 days) | 351 days |
| Buyer's share: 351 × $143.7808 | **$50,467.07** |
| **Check: Seller + Buyer** | **$52,480.00** |

> **Treatment:** Debit to Seller / Credit to Buyer = **$2,012.93** (Lines 509 / 205). The FY 2025–2026 mill rate has not yet been set (per municipal tax status certificate). This proration is an estimate using the current year's tax as a basis. The parties shall reprorate within 90 days after closing when the actual tax bill is issued, per Section 7.7 of the PSA.

---

### Schedule B — Rent Proration (Collected Rents, July 2025)

**Methodology:** Per Section 7.2, collected July 2025 rents are prorated on a per diem basis using 31 days (July). Seller is entitled to 14 days (July 1–14); Buyer is entitled to 17 days (July 15–31). Rents are calculated separately for each lease and aggregated (Sec. 7.2(d)). Uncollected rents (Unit 2E) and vacant units (Unit 3C) are excluded entirely per Section 7.3. Fractional cents are rounded to the nearest cent, with any remaining balance allocated to Buyer.

| Unit | Monthly Rent | July Status | Seller (14/31) | Buyer (17/31) |

>
> **Unit 2E ($1,600.00):** Delinquent for July 2025; excluded from proration per Section 7.3. Buyer assumes the right to collect this delinquent rent post-closing (Sec. 7.3(b)); any amounts collected are first applied to current rents, with excess remitted to Seller within 30 days.
>
> **Unit 3C:** Vacant since 11/1/2024; no rent or security deposit. Estimated market rent of $1,650/mo is informational only and excluded from all prorations.

---

### Schedule C — Security Deposit Transfer

Per Section 7.4, all security deposits held by Seller/Property Manager are transferred to Buyer at closing, reflected as a debit to Seller and credit to Buyer. Residential deposits are held in an interest-bearing escrow account at Harborstone Federal Credit Union per Connecticut law; the commercial deposit is held in a separate non-interest-bearing account per the lease.

If closing is delayed beyond July 20, 2025, updated payoff statements must be obtained and per diem interest added (1st Mortgage: $112.67/day; HELOC: ~$27.93/day; delinquent tax: $13.12/day).

## NOTES

1. **Repair Escrow (Line 516):** The $45,000.00 Repair Escrow is funded solely from Seller's proceeds and held by Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC in a segregated, interest-bearing account pursuant to a separate Repair Escrow Agreement. It is **not** a credit to Buyer and is not counted as part of the Purchase Price. Buyer has 12 months from closing to complete roof repairs and submit paid invoices for reimbursement; any unused balance plus accrued interest returns to Seller. (PSA Sec. 8.4.)

2. **CT DRS Estate Tax Lien:** No monetary payoff is required ($0.00). The estate is below the Connecticut estate tax threshold. A release (CT DRS Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834) has been obtained and will be recorded at Seller's expense (recording fee included in Line 511).

3. **Conveyance Tax (Lines 512 / 900):** The $44,750.00 total ($22,375.00 per party) is an estimate per the title commitment, calculated using the tiered rate structure applicable to the City of Bridgeport. The actual amount will be confirmed upon preparation and filing of Form OP-236. The 50/50 split between Seller and Buyer applies regardless.

4. **Proration Survival:** All proration and adjustment obligations survive closing for 90 days for purposes of reproration and final settlement per Section 7.7 of the PSA. Estimated items (FY 2025–2026 taxes, utility charges if final readings are unavailable) shall be readjusted when final figures become available.

5. **Items marked "to be confirmed" (Lines 200a, 203a, 507a):** Accrued interest on earnest money and on residential security deposits is not quantified in the closing file. These amounts, when confirmed by the Escrow Agent and Property Manager respectively, shall be added as credits to Buyer and corresponding debits to Seller, reducing Buyer's cash required at closing and Seller's net proceeds by equal amounts. See accompanying Notes Memo for details.

Prorations are calculated as of 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on July 15, 2025, with the Closing Date allocated to Buyer, on a 365-day year basis unless otherwise specified. This statement is subject to adjustment for any items marked "to be confirmed" and for reproration pursuant to Section 7.7 of the Purchase & Sale Agreement.*"}

| **Reimbursement Value** | **180 gal. × $3.85 = $693.00** |

> **Treatment:** Per Section 7.6, Buyer reimburses Seller for heating oil remaining in the tank at Seller's last delivered price: **Credit to Seller / Debit to Buyer = $693.00** (Lines 401 / 101). The gauge reading was verified by the licensed inspector (Soundview Property Inspections LLC) and independently confirmed by tank tapping. The oil delivery receipt (Shoreline Fuel & Oil Co., Ticket No. SFO-25-04872) is appended to the pre-closing inspection report.

---

## RECONCILIATION SUMMARY

### Funds Received at Closing

| Source | Amount |
|---|---:|
| Cash from Buyer (Line 302) | $1,083,564.52 |
| Loan Proceeds — Tidewater Savings Bank | $2,640,000.00 |
| Earnest Money (held by Escrow Agent) | $195,000.00 |
| **Total Funds Received** | **$3,918,564.52** |

### Funds Disbursed at Closing

| Disbursement | Amount |
|---|---:|
| Net Proceeds to Seller (Line 602) | $2,864,663.83 |
| Lien Payoffs (Harborstone 1st Mortgage, HELOC, Northbridge) | $866,631.89 |
| Delinquent Property Tax Payoff (City of Bridgeport) | $28,602.80 |
| Repair Escrow (segregated, interest-bearing — held by Escrow Agent) | $45,000.00 |
| Buyer's Settlement Charges (paid to third parties) | $67,074.00 |
| Seller's Settlement Charges (paid to third parties) | $46,592.00 |
| **Total Funds Disbursed** | **$3,918,564.52** |

### Reconciliation

| | Amount |
|---|---:|
| Total Funds Received | $3,918,564.52 |
| Total Funds Disbursed | $3,918,564.52 |
| **Difference** | **$0.00 — RECONCILED** |

### Equity Verification (Lender Requirement)

| Component | Amount |
|---|---:|
| Earnest Money applied to purchase price | $195,000.00 |
| Cash from Buyer at settlement | $1,083,564.52 |
| **Total Buyer Equity** | **$1,278,564.52** |
| Lender minimum equity requirement ($3,900,000 − $2,640,000) | $1,260,000.00 |
| **Equity surplus** | **$18,564.52** |

---

## DISBURSEMENT SUMMARY (WIRES TO BE SENT AT CLOSING)

| Payee | Purpose | Amount |
|---|---|---:|
| Estate of Gerald T. Whitford (Claudia Whitford-Barnes, Executrix) | Net proceeds | $2,864,663.83 |
| Harborstone Federal Credit Union | 1st Mortgage payoff (Loan No. 2015-MTG-008174) | $687,412.33 |
| Harborstone Federal Credit Union | HELOC payoff (Loan No. 2018-HEL-003291) | $148,219.56 |
| Northbridge Construction Co. | Mechanic's lien settlement | $31,000.00 |
| City of Bridgeport — Tax Collector | Delinquent property tax + interest | $28,602.80 |
| Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC (Repair Escrow Account) | Roof repair holdback (segregated) | $45,000.00 |
| Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC | Title charges, recording, conveyance tax, search fees | (per settlement charges) |
| Tidewater Savings Bank | Loan origination fee, tax service fee | $26,485.00 |
| CoreLogic Flood Services | Flood certification | $25.00 |
| Ridgeline Law Group PLLC | Buyer's attorney fees | $12,500.00 |
| Ashford, Clement & Paige LLP | Seller's attorney fees | $11,000.00 |
| Bayshore Management Co. | Management agreement termination fee | $4,500.00 |
| Fairfield County Probate Court | Probate Court Certificate | $150.00 |

> **Lien Payoff Good-Through Dates:** Harborstone 1st Mortgage and HELOC payoffs are good through July 20, 2025. The closing date (July 15, 2025) falls within the good-through period, so no per diem interest adjustment is required. The municipal tax payoff is also valid through July 20, 2025. If closing is delayed beyond July 20, 2025, updated payoff statements must be obtained and per diem interest added (1st Mortgage: $112.67/day; HELOC: ~$27.93/day; delinquent tax: $13.12/day).

---

## NOTES

1. **Repair Escrow (Line 516):** The $45,000.00 Repair Escrow is funded solely from Seller's proceeds and held by Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC in a segregated, interest-bearing account pursuant to a separate Repair Escrow Agreement. It is **not** a credit to Buyer and is not counted as part of the Purchase Price. Buyer has 12 months from closing to complete roof repairs and submit paid invoices for reimbursement; any unused balance plus accrued interest returns to Seller. (PSA Sec. 8.4.)

2. **CT DRS Estate Tax Lien:** No monetary payoff is required ($0.00). The estate is below the Connecticut estate tax threshold. A release (CT DRS Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834) has been obtained and will be recorded at Seller's expense (recording fee included in Line 511).

3. **Conveyance Tax (Lines 512 / 900):** The $44,750.00 total ($22,375.00 per party) is an estimate per the title commitment, calculated using the tiered rate structure applicable to the City of Bridgeport. The actual amount will be confirmed upon preparation and filing of Form OP-236. The 50/50 split between Seller and Buyer applies regardless.

4. **Proration Survival:** All proration and adjustment obligations survive closing for 90 days for purposes of reproration and final settlement per Section 7.7 of the PSA. Estimated items (FY 2025–2026 taxes, utility charges if final readings are unavailable) shall be readjusted when final figures become available.

5. **Items marked "to be confirmed" (Lines 200a, 203a, 507a):** Accrued interest on earnest money and on residential security deposits is not quantified in the closing file. These amounts, when confirmed by the Escrow Agent and Property Manager respectively, shall be added as credits to Buyer and corresponding debits to Seller, reducing Buyer's cash required at closing and Seller's net proceeds by equal amounts. See accompanying Notes Memo for details.

---

## ACKNOWLEDGMENT AND SIGNATURES

The parties hereby acknowledge that the foregoing settlement statement accurately reflects the financial terms of the closing of the sale of 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604, and agree to the debits, credits, prorations, and adjustments set forth herein.

**SELLER:**

ESTATE OF GERALD T. WHITFORD

By: _______________________________

Name: Claudia Whitford-Barnes

Title: Executrix

Date: July 15, 2025

**BUYER:**

MERIDIAN COVE PROPERTIES LLC

By: _______________________________

Name: Elaine R. Matsuda

Title: Manager

Date: July 15, 2025

**CLOSING AGENT:**

PINNACLE ABSTRACT & TITLE LLC

By: _______________________________

Name: Lorraine M. Grasso

Title: Closing Officer

Date: July 15, 2025

---

*This settlement statement is prepared by Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC, Closing Agent, in connection with the closing of the above-referenced transaction. All figures are based on the closing documents delivered to the Closing Agent, including the Purchase & Sale Agreement dated April 22, 2025; the title commitment; lien payoff letters; the municipal tax status certificate; the utility account statement; the pre-closing inspection report; the rent roll and security deposit schedule; the lender commitment letter; the management agreement and termination notice; and the probate fiduciary certificate. Prorations are calculated as of 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on July 15, 2025, with the Closing Date allocated to Buyer, on a 365-day year basis unless otherwise specified. This statement is subject to adjustment for any items marked "to be confirmed" and for reproration pursuant to Section 7.7 of the Purchase & Sale Agreement.*"}
