> **Lien Payoff Good-Through Dates:** Harborstone 1st Mortgage and HELOC payoffs are good through July 20, 2025. The closing date (July 15, 2025) falls within the good-through period, so no per diem interest adjustment is required. The municipal tax payoff is also valid through July 20, 2025. If closing is delayed beyond July 20, 2025, updated payoff statements must be obtained and per diem interest added (1st Mortgage: $112.67/day; HELOC: ~$27.93/day; delinquent tax: $13.12/day).

---

## NOTES

1. **Repair Escrow (Line 516):** The $45,000.00 Repair Escrow is funded solely from Seller's proceeds and held by Pinnacle Abstract & Title LLC in a segregated, interest-bearing account pursuant to a separate Repair Escrow Agreement. It is **not** a credit to Buyer and is not counted as part of the Purchase Price. Buyer has 12 months from closing to complete roof repairs and submit paid invoices for reimbursement; any unused balance plus accrued interest returns to Seller. (PSA Sec. 8.4.)

2. **CT DRS Estate Tax Lien:** No monetary payoff is required ($0.00). The estate is below the Connecticut estate tax threshold. A release (CT DRS Release Certificate No. ETL-2025-08834) has been obtained and will be recorded at Seller's expense (recording fee included in Line 511).

3. **Conveyance Tax (Lines 512 / 900):** The $44,750.00 total ($22,375.00 per party) is an estimate per the title commitment, calculated using the tiered rate structure applicable to the City of Bridgeport. The actual amount will be confirmed upon preparation and filing of Form OP-236. The 50/50 split between Seller and Buyer applies regardless.

4. **Proration Survival:** All proration and adjustment obligations survive closing for 90 days for purposes of reproration and final settlement per Section 7.7 of the PSA. Estimated items (FY 2025–2026 taxes, utility charges if final readings are unavailable) shall be readjusted when final figures become available.

5. **Items marked "to be confirmed" (Lines 200a, 203a, 507a):** Accrued interest on earnest money and on residential security deposits is not quantified in the closing file. These amounts, when confirmed by the Escrow Agent and Property Manager respectively, shall be added as credits to Buyer and corresponding debits to Seller, reducing Buyer's cash required at closing and Seller's net proceeds by equal amounts. See accompanying Notes Memo for details.

6. **FIRPTA / Withholding:** No withholding line item appears on the assumption that the Seller delivers the FIRPTA affidavit / estate equivalent certification required by PSA Section 6.2(i), certifying the sale is exempt from Section 1445 withholding. If that certification is not delivered, up to 15% of the amount realized (~$585,000) may be required to be withheld from Seller's proceeds. See Notes Memo, Item C-15.

---

{"content": "# Response Summary

## Deliverables

Two Word documents prepared for the commercial property closing at 4280 Harborview Boulevard, Bridgeport, CT 06604 (Estate of Gerald T. Whitford → Meridian Cove Properties LLC; closing July 15, 2025):

### 1. `settlement-statement.docx`
A full commercial settlement statement (HUD-1 style) with:
- **Sections 100–300 (Buyer):** Gross amount due $3,967,767.00; credits $2,884,202.48; **cash from Buyer $1,083,564.52**
- **Sections 400–600 (Seller):** Gross due $3,900,693.00; reductions $1,036,029.17; **net proceeds to Seller $2,864,663.83**
- **Settlement charges detail:** title, recording, conveyance tax, loan, attorney/other — Buyer $67,074.00; Seller $46,592.00
- **POC disclosure:** $4,500 appraisal fee (excluded from cash-to-close)
- **Five detailed proration schedules:**
  - Schedule A — Real property taxes (delinquent FY24–25 $28,602.80 from Seller proceeds; FY25–26 estimate prorated 14/351 days = $2,012.93 Seller / $50,467.07 Buyer; assessed value $1,205,330 / 43.54 mills)
  - Schedule B — Rents (per-lease, 14/17 days of 31; $23,600 collected → $12,941.95 to Buyer; Unit 2E delinquent and Unit 3C vacant excluded)
  - Schedule C — Security deposits ($32,400.00 principal transferred)
  - Schedule D — Water/sewer ($1,847.60, billing period ending 7/14, Seller's responsibility)
  - Schedule E — Heating oil (180 gal × $3.85 = $693.00)
- **Reconciliation summary:** Funds in = Funds out = **$3,918,564.52; difference $0.00 — RECONCILED**
- Equity verification, disbursement/wire summary, FIRPTA withholding note, signature block

### 2. `settlement-statement-notes.docx`
A notes memo flagging:
- **9 discrepancies (A-1 to A-9):** title commitment number mismatch; title insurer name inconsistency; lien recording-reference conflicts; utility-bill treatment conflict (PSA vs. municipal lien certificate); first-mortgage per diem rate inconsistency; party address inconsistencies; escrow shortage in payoff; **Letters Testamentary issuance date (Oct 14 vs Oct 11)**; **management agreement effective date (Oct 1 vs Oct 15)**
- **11 assumptions (B-1 to B-11):** unquantified earnest-money interest; unquantified security-deposit interest; estimated conveyance tax; estimated FY25–26 tax; per-lease rent proration methodology; no brokerage; no insurance proration; unspecified survey/env/SNDA costs; possible additional assignment-to-lender recording fee; utility deposits retained by Seller; **appraised value supports loan (no adjustment)**
- **18 confirmation/post-closing items (C-1 to C-18):** payoff good-through dates; CT DRS lien release recording; fiduciary certificate recency; probate certificate; management termination fee; repair escrow/lender requirements; Unit 2E delinquent rent; month-to-month holdover tenancies; commercial lease SNDA; tenant notification; personal guaranty; CPL/gap indemnity; recording sequence; 90-day reproration right; **FIRPTA affidavit/estate certification; Seller's affidavit; Buyer entity documentation; Bill of Sale**
- **Summary table** of financial impact of all unconfirmed items (including FIRPTA withholding up to ~$585,000 if certification not delivered)

## Completeness audit
A full re-read of all 10 source documents was performed. The audit added 2 discrepancies (A-8, A-9), 1 assumption (B-11), 4 confirmation items (C-15 to C-18), and supporting facts (assessed value/mill rate, appraised value, FIRPTA note) that the first draft had omitted. All monetary totals remain intact and reconcile to $0.00. Both files pass ECMA-376 schema validation.

## Source documents reviewed
Purchase & Sale Agreement; Title Commitment; Lender Commitment Letter; Lien Payoff Letters; Bridgeport Tax Lien Statement; Utility Account Summary; Pre-Closing Inspection Report; Rent Roll & Security Deposits (xlsx); Management Agreement & Termination Notice; Probate Fiduciary Certificate.", "file_path": "response.md"}

text:
The completeness audit is complete. Here is what the audit found and fixed:

## Completeness Audit Results

I re-read all 10 source documents and cross-checked every distinct issue against the deliverables. The first draft was solid on monetary items and the original 7 discrepancies / 10 assumptions / 14 confirmation items, but the audit found **7 gaps** that the record supports and the first draft had omitted:

**New discrepancies added to the notes memo:**
- **A-8** — Letters Testamentary issuance date: title commitment says "on or about October 14, 2024" vs. the probate certificate and estate summary say "October 11, 2024"
- **A-9** — Management agreement effective date: rent roll says "October 1, 2024" vs. the management agreement itself says "October 15, 2024"

**New assumption added:**
- **B-11** — Appraised value ($3,920,000) exceeds purchase price, supporting the loan/LTV with no adjustment needed

**New confirmation items added:**
- **C-15** — FIRPTA affidavit / estate equivalent certification (PSA §6.2(i)) — a required Seller delivery with a material settlement impact (up to ~$585,000 withholding if not delivered)
- **C-16** — Seller's affidavit (title commitment Requirement 13)
- **C-17** — Buyer entity documentation (Certificate of Good Standing, operating agreement, member resolution)
- **C-18** — Bill of Sale for personal property (PSA §6.2(b))

**Settlement statement additions:** assessed value ($1,205,330) and mill rate (43.54) supporting the tax figure in Schedule A; appraised value in the transaction summary; a FIRPTA withholding note.

All monetary totals remain intact and reconcile to $0.00. Both `.docx` files were regenerated and pass ECMA-376 schema validation. The notes memo now covers 9 discrepancies, 11 assumptions, and 18 confirmation items, each with its own explicit treatment citing the source document.
